clusters.name: Cards & Payments — Year, Closed, Paid — Closing your account
sample_narratives: On XX/XX/year> I charged seat upgrades for XXXX seats at XXXX XXXX on my card for {$160.00} each. In the past Bank Of America had told me that they only reimurse from certain airlines which included XXXX XXXX, I tried to reach the # at the back of the card for why they did not reimburse me but I was getting a run around and no one could direct me to someone who can take action or tell me why I did not get reimbursed.

American Express closed my business credit card immediately after I paid the balance in full. They gave no advance notice and no valid explanation. The account was in good standing and actively used for legitimate business operations. 

This business card was my only source of working capital. The sudden and unexplained closure caused my business to collapse, resulting in the loss of my income and my ability to operate or cover essential expenses. 

After the closure, I contacted American Express multiple times. Two separate representatives told meon recorded callsthat my payment would be refunded because the account had been paid in full and then immediately shut down. These assurances were repeated more than once. 

Despite these promises, American Express never issued the refund. 

Because they eliminated my business income and withheld the promised refund, I became unable to pay my other American Express account. Instead of correcting their own mistake, American Express sent that account to collections and initiated legal action against me even though their actions directly caused the nonpayment. 

This situation has caused significant financial harm, loss of business income, loss of working capital, emotional stress, and long-term damage to my financial stability.

I follow the instructions to close the account and then I will get a window that pops up saying : Were sorry, please try later When I call, I get the runaround and put on hold and no one comes back. When I logon in order to deactivate and close the account, I get a message in red colour saying that the account has been blocked.

case_records.narrative: There appears to be a serious problem in First National Bank of Omaha 's ( FNBO ) data and/or operations, particularly as it relates to my account. On XX/XX/year>, FNBO wrote to me that it " issued ( me ) a permanent credit in the amount of {$15.00} on XX/XX/year>.... This notice serves as FINAL resolution of your dispute for the above referenced transactions ( XX/XX/year> XXXX XXXX XXXX ). '' Although it took FNBO more than 6 months to acknowledge the correctness of my dispute, I was satisfied that the matter was permanent and final. Disturbingly, FNBO wote to me, dated XX/XX/year>, that it was reposting the XX/XX/year> XXXX XXXX XXXX {$15.00} charge. Thus, FNBO has violated federal law by ignoring its final and permanent investigative decision. There are three possibilities for FNBO 's blatant anti-public action. 1 ) FNBO 's Artificial Intelligence technology has run amok - portending the likely chaotic havoc AI is beginning to have on American society and business. 2 ) The anonymous unsigned FNBO Dispute Department employees in XXXX, XXXX  are complete XXXX who have no idea what they are doing. 3 ) FNBO intentionally retaliates against me because I have filed several complaints

case_records.narrative: American Express simultaneously closed XXXX credit card accounts ( XXXX AMEX  and XXXX ) for reasons that were a mystery to me. Both cards have XXXX balance. I later learned AMEX had been trying to reach me to verify income from new employment. They did not send a letter, but tried to reach me by phone, apparently. I usually don't answer the phone from numbers I don't know to avoid telemarketers. I would have provided the updated ( increased ) income information immediately. I have sent multiple letters to AMEX customer service with no reply. I finally reached someone on the phone who explained the income verification issue and that it was too late to fix it. She suggested I reapply for the XXXX AMEX card, which I did. I was denied ( automatically, by the AMEX algorithm ) because of the prior card closure, putting me in an endless loop. I need a human to see the whole picture here including high credit score ( upper XXXX ), high income ( over $ XXXX ), history of no late payments, and XXXX balance to approve a new XXXX XXXX card which would carry my accrued points over. Also, I can't seem to get any information on points earned by the closed credit card.

To whom it may concern : I have three store cards via a company called Synchrony Bank : XXXX, XXXX, and XXXX. All three websites for these cards make it very difficult to log in and view my account activity, as they constantly request that I verify my identity using a phone number I no longer have access to. They do not offer any other methods of verifying my identity. This makes it difficult to make payments and to review my account in order to ensure fraud has not occurred. 

There must be some rule or regulation against this kind of thing. They do not offer sending an email to the address on the account ; they don't have security questions ; etc. Literally no other method to verify who I am other than using the XXXX phone number. The website links when trying to resolve this are cyclical and just point back to XXXX another as well. 

I have called them repeatedly over the last year to try to resolve this, and they claim their hands are tied and there's nothing they can do, that it's just how their website works. case_records.issue: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
case_records.narrative:
I purchased XXXX shoes using Klarnas Pay in 4 service and fully paid the order. I returned the shoes to XXXX, and XXXX issued a refund to Klarna. 

Despite my original bank account being active, unchanged, and in good standing, Klarnas system converted the refund into Klarna credit and applied it to other balances on my account without my authorization.

I contacted Klarna via their app and requested a manual refund to my bank account. They replied that the refund could not be linked to my original payment plan and that the automated system could not be changed. Klarna acknowledged the issue and assigned complaint ID XXXX, but the refund has still not been returned to my bank account. 

This is a technical error on Klarnas side and does not relieve them of the obligation to return my funds to my original payment method, as required under Pay in 4 refund policy.

I have attached proof.

I have Bank of America set up for automatic payments to pay the " Statement Balance '' in full on the due date. Despite having sufficient funds and the autopay scheduled, Bank of America failed to apply the auto-pay appropriately, resulting in an unjustified interest charge of {$44.00} on the subsequent statement. 
I contacted customer service on XX/XX/year> to request a refund of this interest fee. The request was denied. The representative stated that their automated system does not consistently display the scheduled payment amount/date on the autopay page, nor does it send notifications ( email/text ) to customers confirming when autopay is scheduled for what date nor amount. 
I believe this is an unfair and deceptive practice. The bank is failing to execute autopay for the full statement balance while simultaneously not providing adequate visibility for customers to review, confirm, and keep a record of these payments.

I am filing a formal complaint against Bilt Technologies , Inc. ( Bilt Rewards ) for deceptive promotional practices, false advertising, and executive negligence regarding a financial liability tied to the Bilt Mastercard XXXX. 

I have been trying for weeks to obtain customer service to be ignored or occasionally placated with a few sentences of outdated information.

1. Executive Negligence & Financial Liability : During the transition of my Bilt XXXX from XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, a {$500.00} ledger discrepancy occurred. Bilt Vice President XXXX XXXX explicitly instructed me in writing to " dispose '' of a physical {$500.00} bank-issued refund check from XXXX XXXX , claiming Bilt would reconcile it on the backend. Discarding a legal bank document leaves a {$500.00} discrepancy on a regulated ledger and exposes me to automated clawbacks. I proposed a standard accounting solution to XXXX XXXX : I cash the XXXX XXXX check to XXXX  out their ledger, and Bilt charges my new card {$500.00} to offset their digital credit. Following this proposal, XXXX XXXX completely ceased communication, willfully ignoring weeks of follow up calls and emails and leaving my account in financial limbo.

case_records.narrative:
I am filing this complaint regarding several recent changes to the Bilt credit card and rewards program that were implemented without adequate notice, as well as the inability to obtain support from the company to resolve the issue. 

Historically, I used my Bilt credit card to pay my monthly rent. The rent charge would appear on my credit card statement, and I would pay the balance when my statement was due, just as with any other credit card purchase. Recently, Bilt changed its system so that rent payments are no longer processed through the credit card and are instead directly debited from a linked checking account. 

I was not notified of this change in advance. I only discovered the change on XX/XX/XXXX ( rent day ) when the payment process behaved differently than expected. This created confusion and significantly changed the financial utility of the card, as well as completely wiped out my checking account, which I was expecting to happen. 

After encountering this issue, I attempted to contact Bilt customer support. I have now been waiting over 10 days ( 8+ business days ) for any meaningful response. The only support currently available appears to be an automated AI chatbo

The XXXX app is how I purchase my groceries, and normally I am able to add it to XXXX, not for the purpose of transferring money, but to checkout and afford food, and other necessities. 

My card stopped functioning not only on XXXX, but on my bill websites, and every other online purchase. It declined in store as of XX/XX/XXXX as well, and I had to retry the transaction in the hopes it would go through.

There were no further solutions provided to me, and after trying what little help I was offered, my card still remains essentially unusable.

At one point, on XX/XX/XXXX as well, the automated system couldnt even

case_records.sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card
case_records.narrative: To the Account Review Team, I am writing to formally request a manual review and reversal of a {$240.00} interest charge assessed to my XXXX  XXXX Business credit card. Due to a brief administrative oversight, my payment was delayed by only 3 days. As soon as the error was noticed, the balance was immediately paid in full. This account is otherwise kept in excellent standing, and this minor delay is an isolated incident. When I contacted phone support, a manager informed me that the automated system issued a denial. However, as a loyal Bank of America client who holds multiple active XXXX XXXX credit cards, a {$240.00} penalty for a 72-hour oversight severely and unfairly impacts my business expenses. I am a new small business. I kindly request that a human review team override the automated system decision and credit the {$240.00} back to my account as a gesture of goodwill to preserve our ongoing business relationship.Thank you for your time, consideration, and prompt attention to this matter.

assessment.why_concerning: CRITICAL severity: 153 consumers affected by the same pattern at CITIBANK, N.A. and others, and it is accelerating fast (+100% in 7 days). Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: algorithm, artificial intelligence, automated decision, automated loop.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: actions taken without notice, automated adverse actions, automated loops, decisions given with no explanation.
The pattern repeats across 153 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Accounts/cards are actioned without notice, denying customers the chance to respond before harm crystallises.
Automated adverse actions are taken at scale, raising doubt that the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: An automated account-closure / fraud model appears to be exiting customers and reversing payments without manual review or notice.
hypotheses.evidence: 153 complaints showing automated closure and payment-reversal cues; concentrated at CITIBANK, N.A., AMERICAN EXPRESS COMPANY, BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to CITIBANK, N.A. for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask CITIBANK, N.A. directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated monitoring model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

actions.action: Consider commissioning a Skilled Person review of the firm's automated-decisioning governance and oversight.

CFPB COMPLAINT Product : CITI XXXX Credit Card Company : Citibank , N.A . 
Issue : Improper account closure ; inaccurate credit reporting ; failure to honor notice ; failure to resolve dispute WHAT HAPPENED In XXXX of XXXX, Citibank sent me a written notice that my credit card account ending in XXXX would be closed due to inactivity on XX/XX/2025 unless I made a qualifying transaction before that date. 
To keep the account open, I made a purchase on XX/XX/2025, prior to account closure of the stated deadline. Despite my compliance with the notice, Citibank closed the account anyway on XX/XX/2025. 
After noticing the unexpected closure, I contacted Citibank ( date unknown ) and informed them that I had made a qualifying transaction before the deadline. I questioned how my account could be considered inactive when a valid transaction had occurred. 
Citibank representatives acknowledged the closure but refused to reopen the account or correct it, stating that nothing can be done and that I must pay the balance and accept the consequences. No one provided a clear explanation for why the account was closed contrary to the written notice. 
Following the closure, my automatic payment failed because the account had been shut down by Citis mistake. This caused my account to become delinquent, which I did not consent to and which would not have occurred had Citi properly honored the terms of their own notice. 
I contacted Citibank again on Saturday, XX/XX/XXXX, and the representative again claimed there was nothing they could do, despite the fact that the account was closed in error. Over 100 days, Citibank has failed to investigate, failed to correct the issue, and failed to provide any resolution. I also requested All Statements, Missive, Memos, and Notices. I was provided with partial paperwork, statements only, over the allotted 10 day timeline I was given.

Subject : Capital One closed both of my credit card accounts with no notice and refuses to disclose the reason. 

Complaint : Capital One abruptly closed both of my credit card accounts without any prior notice, written correspondence, or adverse action letter. I discovered the closure while traveling in XXXX when my card was declined. I initially assumed the issue was related to travel, but it was not. 

I immediately contacted Capital One multiple times by phone and in person at a Capital One XXXX location. My case was escalated several times, but no one provided any explanation for the closures. I was repeatedly told they could not see the reason or could not share it. This violates the Equal Credit Opportunity Act ( Regulation B ), which requires a creditor to provide a clear, specific reason for account closure within 30 days. 

My accounts were in good standing. I have never missed payments and I regularly paid my balances in full. At the time of closure my balance was higher than normal due to international travel, but still current and paid on time.

The account was never used, there is no balance owed, and Capital One has completed whatever internal review they claim to have. There is no reason for this delay.

case_records.sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card
case_records.narrative:
On XX/XX/year>, I received an adverse action notice from Capital One denying my request for credit. The specific denial reason provided was : " Capital One has observed activity on one or more of your Capital One accounts that is inconsistent with typical customer account usage '' This denial is unfair and relies on outdated internal data rather than my current creditworthiness. The " activity '' referenced refers to a single secured credit card account from over four years ago. That account is closed, and I do not owe Capital One any money.

Since that time, I have successfully rebuilt my credit profile and established a successful small business. Despite my current high credit score and clean history with other lenders, Capital One is using a " lifetime ban '' algorithm based on a resolved, 4-year-old secured account to automatically deny me services.

By refusing to look at my actual credit report and instead relying on a permanent internal " blacklist, '' the bank is failing to assess my true creditworthiness.

case_records.narrative:
I applied for a capital one platinum credit card on Friday XXXX XXXX. I was approved. XXXX  days later XXXX XXXX. I received an email stating my credit card account was closed. The later stated We are closing this account because Capital One has observed activity on one or more of your Capital One accounts that is inconsistent with typical customer account usage. 
Please stop using this account immediately and destroy any cards and checks associated with it. 
Also, you should update any recurring payments you have set up using this account, such as gym memberships, subscriptions to online streaming services, etc. 
You are still responsible for paying the full amount of any remaining account balance and you'll continue to receive billing statements until the balance reaches XXXX. 

I did not request a request and was in fact provided a virtual card until I receive the new one. I contacted capital one to ask what inconsistent activity was observed on my account, as I never used the card in the 2 days time span. They were unable to provide a reason. Now I have a hard inquiry and a closed account on my credit report with no explanation.

Complainant Information : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX, PA XXXX Phone : XXXX ( XXXX ) XXXX Email : XXXX I. INTRODUCTION I am submitting this formal legal complaint regarding the unexpected closure of my Bank of America credit card account without prior notice, without a required adverse action notice, and without a clear explanation for the decision. The account was closed without warning, leaving me unaware of the exact date the closure occurred. 
XXXX. FACTUAL BACKGROUND I discovered that my credit card account had been closed without any prior notification.

The bank has implicitly admitted fault regarding this incident by reversing the late fee associated with this error. However, they continue to report the " Late Payment '' to the credit bureaus.

It is an unfair and deceptive practice to provide a consumer with a " Thank You '' confirmation, silently reverse it due to a system error, fail to provide notice of the reversal, and then penalize the consumer 's credit report for the resulting delay. case_records.narrative:
On XX/XX/year>, an initial payment was attempted on a new credit card account. Due to a manual data entry error, an incorrect bank account number was submitted. This resulted in a returned payment notification. 

I immediately identified the clerical error, updated the banking information with the correct account details, and successfully processed the full payment before the scheduled due date. There is no outstanding balance or history of delinquency on this account. 

Despite the successful on-time payment and the proactive correction of the data entry error, the company has placed a mandatory 90-day freeze on the credit line. Customer service representatives state that this is an automated system flag that can not be reviewed or overridden by human agents. 

I am requesting a manual review of this risk flag by the XXXX XXXX or XXXX department. Maintaining a three-month account freeze for a corrected technical errorwhere no financial loss or late payment occurredis an unreasonable restriction of service. I request that the freeze be lifted immediately.

case_records.sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card
case_records.narrative:
I opened a credit card account with Chase, but I never received the physical credit card. 

Over a period of more than 6 weeks, I contacted the bank multiple times by phone and visited the branch in person. Each time I was told to wait, and no one was able to provide any clear information or solution. 

The card was never delivered, and I was unable to use the account at all. Despite this, no proper action was taken by the bank to resolve the issue. 

The branch staff, including the assistant branch manager, stated that they could not help and had no information about the status of the card. I was repeatedly told to wait without any results. 

This situation caused me a loss of time and unnecessary stress. Additionally, the hard credit inquiry related to this account negatively impacted my credit score, even though I never used the account or received the card. 

The bank failed to provide basic service and failed to resolve a simple issue over an extended period of time.

case_records.narrative:
I am filing a complaint regarding a credit card account opened with Total Visa. 

The account required an upfront payment of {$99.00} to open, which was paid. However, the physical credit card has never been received. Despite this, the company has issued a bill stating that {$41.00} is past due. 

I have attempted to resolve this issue by contacting the company, but I am unable to access the account through their automated system because I do not have a card number. Their phone system requires an account number to proceed, and I have been unable to reach a live representative. 

I also attempted to set up online account access, but this was not possible without the card information. I have contacted the company via email requesting cancellation of the account and assistance, but have not received a resolution. 

It is unreasonable for the company to charge fees and report a past-due balance on an account where the card was never delivered and access to the account has been effectively blocked.

RE : Citibank Billing Dispute Resolution Compliance Failure Citibank Reference Number : XXXX Original Disputed Amount : {$1100.00} | Credited Amount : {$1000.00} Remaining Unresolved Balance Gap : {$19.00} plus all accrued interest and finance fees I am filing a formal complaint against Citibank for a billing error calculation failure under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ). Citibank recently sent me an official letter under Reference Number XXXX stating they received a credit from the merchant for {$1000.00} and marked my fraud investigation as " complete. '' However, the original fraudulent international transaction total charged to my card was {$1100.00}. 

Due to international merchant arbitrage and foreign currency conversion fluctuations, the merchant 's manual partial refund left a {$19.00} balance gap on my account. Citibank 's automated system has since reversed my temporary conditional credit, leaving my subsequent statement balance completely incorrect and exposing me to backdated interest charges on a fraudulent transaction. 

The international merchant is a master manipulator who never manufactured or delivered the custom merchandise, committed a severe data breach

assessment.why_concerning: HIGH severity: 142 consumers affected by the same pattern at CAPITAL ONE FINANCIAL CORPORATION and others, and it is accelerating fast (+50% in 7 days). Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: algorithm, artificial intelligence, automated decision, automated loop.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: actions taken without notice, automated adverse actions, automated loops, decisions given with no explanation.
The pattern repeats across 142 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Accounts/cards are actioned without notice, denying customers the chance to respond before harm crystallises.
Automated adverse actions are taken at scale, raising doubt that the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: An automated account-closure / fraud model appears to be exiting customers and reversing payments without manual review or notice.
hypotheses.evidence: 142 complaints showing automated closure and payment-reversal cues; concentrated at CAPITAL ONE FINANCIAL CORPORATION, CITIBANK, N.A., BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION.

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to CAPITAL ONE FINANCIAL CORPORATION for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask CAPITAL ONE FINANCIAL CORPORATION directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated monitoring model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

actions.action: Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

XX/XX/year> Thursday - attempted to withdraw {$480.00} from an ATM of First Hawaiian Bank inside a XXXX XXXX  at XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX ) ( You can call them for ATM Number ) at around XXXX that day in XXXX, HI XXXX When I tried to withdraw that amount, even-thou I had over a XXXX in savings, it said transaction declined, so I waited a few minutes and tried again for a lesser amount of {$420.00} and that worked which I was trying to get half of my rent money, did not notice anything wrong, the next morning, about to go get the other half of my rent money and checked my cell for my bank info and to my shock, the {$480.00} that was declined the day before was taken out of my savings account, I never got the {$480.00} and OBVIOUS ATM MISTAKE because where I live, those ATM 's ONLY ALLOW a limit of {$500.00} withdrawals a DAY, so HOW could they ( My Bank ) ALLOW me to what looked like withdrawal from an ATM amount of {$900.00}, NOW WAY!!, inside the bank for sure , Drive thru yes, but ATM- NO!!, called the bank and some guy maybe from not where I live told me ATM 's could do XXXX withdrawals a day, WHICH is not TRUE cause it says Right on the Monitor of the ATM " {$500.00} Limit a Day Withdrawals '', he then sent me to another dept. the Dispute Dept., talked to a women and at first she told me might take 10 to 30 days to correct this, then I told her my rent due in a few days, she then said she will talk to her supervisor and try to escalate issue quicker, and they will call me, so far NOBODY has called Me and they still holding my {$480.00} which they messed up on causing me grief thru that ATM I used, my rent is due soon and they holding my money forcing me to use other funds to pay other bills causing me potential financial grief.... computer error, really HOW LONG CAN IT TAKE TO FIX THAT!! assessment.why_concerning: HIGH severity: 60 consumers affected by the same pattern at CITIBANK, N.A. and others, and it is accelerating fast (+100% in 7 days). Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: algorithm, artificial intelligence, automated system, chatbot.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: actions taken without notice, automated adverse actions, automated loops, decisions given with no explanation.
The pattern repeats across 60 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Accounts/cards are actioned without notice, denying customers the chance to respond before harm crystallises.
Automated adverse actions are taken at scale, raising doubt that the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: The firm likely tightened an automated transaction-monitoring / financial-crime model, raising false-positive exit rates — legitimate customers are 'de-risked' and offboarded without manual review.
hypotheses.evidence: 60 complaints showing account-closure and suspicious-activity language concentrated at a few firms; concentrated at CITIBANK, N.A., BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, Chime Financial Inc.

hypotheses.mechanism: An automated fraud-detection model may be flagging legitimate transactions, freezing funds before any human check.
hypotheses.evidence: 60 complaints showing fund-freeze and access-loss cues; concentrated at CITIBANK, N.A., BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, Chime Financial Inc.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to CITIBANK, N.A. for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask CITIBANK, N.A. directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated monitoring model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

clusters.name: Banking — Chime, Dispute, Balance — Managing an account
sample_narratives: On XX/XX/XXXX, multiple fraudulent transactions were attempted on my account from the same merchant ( XXXX, TN ). Several attempts were declined by Bank of America, which proves these were unauthorized and part of a fraud pattern. If I had authorized one of these charges, I would have authorized all of them but I did not authorize ANY of the transactions. The system flagged the others as fraud, yet Bank of America incorrectly concluded that the {$500.00} transaction was approved by me. This is inconsistent and violates Regulation Es requirement for a reasonable investigation.Bank of America denied my initial claim and then denied my appeal, stating that the transaction was authorized by me or someone with permission to use my card. This is incorrect. No one else has permission to use my card or account, and I did not authorize this transaction.

In XX/XX/year>, I disputed a single charge in the amount of {$450.00}. The dispute was processed one time. When the merchant did not comply, the {$450.00} was removed from my account. I accepted this outcome and took no further action. 
In XX/XX/year>, Navy Federal Credit Union issued a temporary credit of {$450.00}, which is clearly reflected on my account statements as a reversal. At no time after this reversal was I notified of an overpayment, duplicate credit, or outstanding balance related to this dispute. 
In XX/XX/year>, my accountan account that has never previously been overdrawnwas suddenly debited and reflected a negative balance of approximately {$900.00}. Upon contacting Navy Federal Credit Union, I was informed by a representative that the negative balance was the result of a system glitch related to the XX/XX/year> dispute. 
I immediately questioned how an overpayment could exist when : I disputed the charge only once, I never received duplicate or excess funds, and any alleged overpayment should have been identified and corrected at the time of the XX/XX/year> reversal. 
No explanation was provided for the delayed action, nor was I given prior notice before the funds were withdrawn. 
Since XXXX, all deposits made to my account have been automatically seized to offset this balance, causing ongoing financial harm. I am a single mother of XXXX young children, and this unexpected and unexplained account action has directly affected my ability to pay for essential living expenses. The stress and instability caused by this situation have been significant and ongoing.

I am being held financially responsible for an internal processing error that I did not cause, did not benefit from, and was not informed of in a timely manner. This action appears inconsistent with fair billing, error-resolution, and notification requirements.

Background I was previously enrolled in Discovers Balance+ feature and relied on it for ordinary household cash-flow management, including rent. I used the feature as intended and repaid the Balance+ amount immediately, restoring my account to a positive balance. At no time did I misuse the feature or leave my account overdrawn.

At the time, I was on medical leave from my primary employment and received consistent weekly income of approximately {$400.00} {$600.00} through a gig platform. That income was deposited by ACH into my Discover savings account. I attempted to route this same income to my Discover checking account in order to meet Balance+ eligibility requirements ; however, Discover would not accept this income source into checking, even though it accepted the identical income into savings.

Formal Complaint Against BMO Bank N.A .

Issue : Improper Account Freeze and Unlawful Detention of Validated Cash Funds. 
Details : On XX/XX/year>, I attempted to withdraw a cash deposit of {$1000.00} at the BMO branch located at XXXX IL. During the transaction, a BMO employee actively instructed me to repeatedly attempt/resubmit the transaction. This repetitive activity, explicitly directed by bank staff, triggered an automated fraud flag. BMO has subsequently frozen my account and stated they will hold my entire cash balance for up to 90 business days during an account closure.

Regulatory Violation : Per BMO 's published Deposit Account Agreement ( Section 8, Funds Availability ), funds from cash deposits made in person with a BMO banker must be made available on the same business day. case_records.issue: Problem caused by your funds being low
case_records.narrative:
To Whom It May Concern, I am writing to formally dispute a continuous pattern of excessive, compounding, and predatory automated overdraft/NSF fees applied to my checking account ( ending in XXXX ) between XX/XX/2026, and XX/XX/2026. I have attached the complete 60-day transaction history highlighting these specific errors and am requesting a comprehensive audit and courtesy reversal by a corporate manager.

I attempted to resolve this locally by speaking with the manager at the XXXX XXXX. The manager refused to help me, dismissively told me I was wrong, and stated that everything processed by the bank was correct. She claimed that the fees are triggered because I am being charged based on the total negative balance of the account rather than per individual transaction item.

While I now understand that your automated system utilizes the total negative balance at the end of the day to override individual item protections, the way these charges stack up creates an inescapable trap. Please review the following blatant issues clearly visible in the attached screenshots : Predatory Double-Charging on Single Returned Items ( XX/XX/1314 ) : On XX/XX/XXXX, a {$600.00} Chase transfer attem

assessment.why_concerning: MEDIUM severity: 53 consumers affected by the same pattern at Chime Financial Inc and others, and it is accelerating fast (+100% in 7 days). Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: algorithm, automated loop, automated system, chatbot.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: actions taken without notice, automated adverse actions, automated loops, decisions given with no explanation.
The pattern repeats across 53 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Accounts/cards are actioned without notice, denying customers the chance to respond before harm crystallises.
Automated adverse actions are taken at scale, raising doubt that the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: The firm likely tightened an automated transaction-monitoring / financial-crime model, raising false-positive exit rates — legitimate customers are 'de-risked' and offboarded without manual review.
hypotheses.evidence: 53 complaints showing account-closure and suspicious-activity language concentrated at a few firms; concentrated at Chime Financial Inc, BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, Block, Inc..

hypotheses.mechanism: An automated fraud-detection model may be flagging legitimate transactions, freezing funds before any human check.
hypotheses.evidence: 53 complaints showing fund-freeze and access-loss cues; concentrated at Chime Financial Inc, BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, Block, Inc..

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to Chime Financial Inc for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask Chime Financial Inc directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated monitoring model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

sample_narratives:
XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX purports to have extended credit to XXXX XXXX XXXX through credit card account XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX in XX/XX/XXXX. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX executed a Limited Power of Attorney appointing me as Attorney-in-Fact to handle all credit, financial, debt collection, and consumer reporting matters on his behalf. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, I filed CFPB Complaint No. XXXX as Attorney-in-Fact for XXXX XXXX XXXX, documenting systemic fraud by Equifax Inc., Fair Isaac Corporation ( FICO ) XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX and XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX 
The complaint documented that on XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX formally reserved the name " Equifax Inc. '' with the XXXX  Secretary of Statea preemptive action demonstrating clear evidence of premeditation to evade legal accountability for mounting FTC antitrust charges. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, Equifax Inc. was incorporated in XXXX  as a separate entitya deliberate act by XXXX XXXX XXXX to manipulate the corporate record and fabricate a false appearance of continuity. This incorporation was never intended to operate independently but to act as a placeholder for XXXX 's later filings. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX amended its Articles of Incorporation to adopt the name " Equifax Inc. '' a superficial rebranding designed to distance the company from its tarnished reputation. 
On the same day, XX/XX/XXXX, the XX/XX/XXXX Equifax Inc. ( the separate entity incorporated 11 months earlier ) changed its name to " XXXX XXXX XXXX. '' This contradictory and circular name swap created two conflicting corporate identities existing in tandemfor a split second on XX/XX/XXXX, two entities were both called " Equifax Inc. '' simultaneouslyan unlawful duplication of names in direct violation of XXXX  XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. 
This reckless name-swapping maneuver compromised XXXX 's legal continuity and introduced a fatal defect into its corporate record, effectively transforming the entity into a legally defective construct under XX

CFPB COMPLAINT LETTER INACCURACIES ON MY CREDIT REPORTS Subject : FCRA Violations Inaccurate, Unverified, and Incomplete Information Reported by Experian, Equifax, and TransUnion Dear Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, I am filing a complaint because multiple credit reporting agencies are reporting inaccurate, incomplete, and unverified information on my credit files in violation of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ). Despite previous disputes, these items continue to be reported incorrectly. 
Below is a detailed breakdown of the inaccuracies for each bureau : EXPERIAN INACCURACIES 1. XXXX  XXXX XXXX XXXX ( {$500.00} ) Experian reports a charge-off without providing the Date of First Delinquency ( DOFD ).

Missing DOFD violates FCRA 623 ( a ) ( 5 ).

The account previously showed a natural disaster remark online and was removed without explanation, showing inconsistent reporting. 
XXXX. XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( {$690.00} ) Experian continues reporting this collection even though the collector has provided no debt validation. 
Reporting as verified without proof violates FCRA 611 and 607 ( b ). 
XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  XXXX XXXX XXXX ( {$1800.00} ) Experian labels the account as Consumer disputes after resolution even though no validation was ever provided.

This is inaccurate and misleading, violating FCRA 607 ( b ).

These should have been removed under FCRA 609 ( a ) and 611.

DOFD appears inconsistent.

This means the collection agency is reporting a false date and re-aging the debt, which violates FCRA 623 ( a ) ( 5 ). When a debt is sold, the original creditor must report a XXXX balance. Instead, both the original creditor and XXXX XXXX are reporting the same balance, which results in duplicate reporting and an inaccurate representation of the debt.

case_records.product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
case_records.narrative: I am filling this complaint under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) due to the willful noncompliance of the credit bureaus and data furnishers in properly investigating my disputes, as required under 15 U.S.C 1681i and 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ( b ). Despite my repeated submissions of disputes containing sufficient documentation to demonstrate inaccuracies, the credit bureaus have failed to conduct a reasonable reinvestigation and have continued to report incorrect information on my credit report. Furthermore the data furnishers have neglected their legal obligation to investigate the disputed information when notified by the credit bureaus. Recently, the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau filed a lawsuit against Transunion for it's failure to properly investigate consumer disputes, citing " sham investigations '' and other unlawful practices. These practices, as outlined by the CFPB, include : Conducting inadequate investigations that fail to address consumer disputes properly. Reinserting previously disputed and inaccurate information without explanation. Failing to notify customers about investigation results effectively. Please review the inaccurate information being report ; X

case_records.narrative:
Carrington Mortgage Services incorrectly reported a XXXX day late payment for XXXX of XXXX through XX/XX/XXXX ( XXXX total months ) on my FHA mortgage. At that time, my loan was under a CARES Act COVID-19 forbearance review and approval, during which I was advised in writing that no late payments would be reported. 

Carrington initially confirmed my eligibility for forbearance. Without explanation or borrower notification, the account was later converted to a partial claim. I was not informed of the reason for this change, and no notice was provided indicating that delinquency reporting would occur during this transition.

Despite this, Carrington reported the account as delinquent. This reporting is inaccurate and noncompliant with CARES Act requirements for federally backed mortgage loans. 

I have submitted a formal Notice of Error under RESPA requesting correction. This inaccurate reporting has caused material financial harm, including preventing me from completing an FHA cash-out refinance to consolidate high interest tuition payments and pay off student debt.

case_records.narrative:
I am writing to formally file a complaint regarding an improperly reported collection account that appears on my credit report. The account was reported approximately ten ( 10 ) months after I moved out of the apartment, without any prior written notice, billing statement, or opportunity to dispute the alleged debt, which is contrary to federal law.

At no point after vacating the apartment was I notified of any outstanding balance. I did not receive : A final account statement A demand for payment A notice of intent to place the account into collections Any communication from the original creditor or a collection agency Despite this lack of notice, the account was suddenly reported as a collection, which caused unjustified damage to my credit profile. 

Under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) and Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ), consumers are entitled to proper notification and validation of a debt before negative information is furnished to the credit bureaus. Reporting a collection account without first providing notice or an opportunity to resolve or dispute the alleged debt is improper and unlawful.

case_records.product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
case_records.narrative:
Against : Experian Information Solutions, LLC ; TransUnion LLC ; Equifax Information Services LLC Consumer : XXXX XXXX XXXX Product : Credit Reporting / Consumer Reports Issue : Incorrect Information on Credit Report / Identity Theft / Failure to Block Fraudulent Data SUMMARY OF COMPLAINT I am a documented victim of identity theft and am exercising my rights under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), including 15 U.S.C. 1681c-2 ( Section 605B ), 1681e ( b ), and 1681i.

Despite receiving my FTC Identity Theft Report ( No. XXXX, sworn XX/XX/XXXX ), proof of identity, and identification of fraudulent accounts and inquiries, Experian, TransUnion, and Equifax each failed to timely block fraudulent information within the four-business-day window required by federal law. 
Instead, the bureaus issued contradictory responses, relied on unsupported furnisher assertions, and continued publishing or processing disputed data that caused significant and unexplained credit score reductions. 
IDENTITY THEFT AND MULTI-STATE FRAUD INDICATORS There is documented evidence of fraudulent activity involving my name and Social Security number across multiple states, including California and Georgia.

case_records.issue: Problem with a company's investigation into an existing problem
case_records.narrative:
I am writing to dispute an incorrect charge-off listing reported under my name for the account XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  According to the credit report I received, this account shows a balance of {$0.00} and is marked as " tied up. '' However, there has been no explanation or documentation provided to justify why this account is being reported as a charge-off. As a consumer, I have the right to dispute inaccurate or incomplete information under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), specifically 15 U.S.C. 1681i, which requires credit bureaus to ensure that information on a credit report is accurate and verifiable. Given the ambiguous nature of the charge-off entry, I request a full investigation into this matter to clarify the account 's status and to ensure the information reported is correct.

Upon closer examination, I noticed that the charge-off was reported without any prior formal notification or detailed breakdown of the balance. According to 15 U.S.C. 1681c, creditors are obligated to report accurate information within a reasonable timeframe. The charge-off status, without adequate verification or supporting documentation, is causing me significant concern. Furth

case_records.narrative:
I am submitting this second complaint because the original responses I received from the credit reporting agencies ( TransUnion, Experian, and Equifax ) were inadequate, incomplete, and in violation of multiple consumer protection laws. 

In my first complaint, I provided specific documentation and legal references requesting : The deletion or correction of inaccurate and unverifiable negative accounts Proof of permissible purpose to report and share my credit data Verification through original signed contracts, not just data entries from furnishers Assurance that my personal data was not compromised, especially in light of known data breaches ( XXXX TransUnion ) However, all three agencies failed to meet their obligations under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) and related statutes : They did not provide documentation verifying the disputed debts They relied on furnishers unverifiable responses without reviewing my evidence They failed to conduct a reasonable reinvestigation, as required by FCRA 1681i They ignored the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act ( GLBA ) by not disclosing who accessed my data or how it was safeguarded They continued to report damaging and incorrect information tha

case_records.product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
case_records.narrative: I previously contacted TransUnion and spoke directly with an agent who assured me that a dispute had been properly filed and that the issue was being handled ( XXXX, Account # XXXX ). During that conversation, I made it very clear that this account was reported without my consent and that I had already submitted a police report confirming identity theft, along with a prior CFPB complaint documenting the same issue. I relied on those assurances and waited several weeks, expecting TransUnion to comply with its legal obligations. When I reviewed my credit report again, the same disputed account was still being reported as if no investigation had occurred. This directly contradicts what I was told and reflects a failure by TransUnion to conduct a reasonable reinvestigation as required under the Fair Credit Reporting Act. TransUnion had actual notice of identity theft and supporting documentation, yet failed to block or remove this account as required by 15 USC 1681c-2. By continuing to report this account, TransUnion is violating its duty to ensure maximum possible accuracy under 15 USC 1681e ( b ) and its obligation to properly reinvestigate disputed information under 15 USC 1681i. case_records.narrative: This is my fifth complaint after disputing these accounts continuously for over XXXX months with full documentation submitted every single time. Instead of conducting a lawful reinvestigation, the credit bureaus have engaged in a pattern of delay, deflection, and premature complaint closures. I am repeatedly told that I am working with a third party, despite submitting disputes directly myself, and despite the bureaus acknowledging receipt of my documentation. Most recently, my complaints have been closed by alleging potential fraud without explanation, without evidence, and without any completed investigation. This is especially concerning given the bureaus well-documented history of multiple data breaches, which raises a legitimate question as to whether my information was compromised due to their own failures. If fraud truly exists, it must be clearly identified, documented, and reflected accurately on my file. Instead, the bureaus continue reporting inaccurate late payments, charge-offs, and inquiries as verified while refusing to produce any actual verification such as payment posting records, billing statements, or internal account ledgers. case_records.narrative:
I am the victim of identity theft. My consumer credit file contains multiple accounts that I did not open, did not authorize, did not benefit from, and have no legal or factual connection to whatsoever. These accounts are the direct result of criminal identity theft and fraud. 

I timely submitted an FTC Identity Theft Report along with supporting documentation clearly establishing that these accounts are fraudulent. Despite receiving legally sufficient proof, you failed to conduct a lawful, reasonable, and good-faith investigation as required under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), and you failed to delete the fraudulent tradelines within the mandatory statutory timeframe. 

Your continued reporting of this false and damaging information constitutes willful non-compliance with the FCRA. Once an account is identified as the result of identity theft and supported by an FTC Identity Theft Report, the law is unequivocal : the information must be blocked and removed. There is no discretion, no delay, and no justification for continued reporting. 

Your actions and omissions have caused substantial financial harm, reputational damage, denial of credit opportunities, emotional dist

I am filing this complaint because Equifax mishandled my identity-theft dispute under FCRA 605B and created multiple barriers that prevented me from properly disputing. I originally submitted an FBI XXXX  Complaint during the federal shutdown, and Equifax should have treated it as valid identity-theft documentation. Instead, they handled it as a standard investigation with no urgency, no blocking, and no 605B process.

This CFPB complaint is my escalation and includes my newly filed FTC Identity Theft Report, created today now that the government shutdown no longer prevents FTC submissions.

Even after receiving the XXXX report, Equifax refused to block identity-theft items and issued responses claiming that fraudulent inquiries were statements of fact. No Method of Verification was provided for any item, despite requiring MOV under FCRA 1681i ( a ) ( 6 ) ( B ) ( iii ). I also received a vague refusal-to-block with no explanation.

case_records.narrative:
I disputed a credit card account and XXXX unauthorized business inquiries that are being reported on my consumer credit report. The credit bureau responded that the creditor certified the account information as accurate, but they did not provide any explanation of how they verified it or what documents were used. Under the Fair Credit Reporting Act, a consumer has the right to receive a description of the verification procedure and copies of the documentation relied upon, but this information was not provided to me even though I specifically requested it. 
In my dispute, I asked the bureau to show how the account was verified and to provide the documentation, including any signed agreement. They did not provide any proof or documents, and the disputed account remains on my credit report. 
Additionally, XXXX business credit inquiries are appearing on my consumer credit report. These inquiries were not authorized, and no written consent was given for my consumer credit file to be accessed for business purposes. I requested that these inquiries be removed unless the bureau could provide proof of authorization, but they remain on my report with no explanation or evidence provided.

case_records.issue: Problem with a company's investigation into an existing problem
case_records.narrative:
Permissible Purpose : The law says no one can report information about me without a permissible purpose and my authorization. These accounts and inquiries have neither. There's no written agreement, no verifiable consent, nothing proving I authorized them. Continuing to report them is unlawful. On XX/XX/year>, I sent a formal dispute to TransUnion about unauthorized and inaccurate information on my credit report. I wasn't just making a casual complaint- I sent them everything they needed, including my FTC Report ( Report ID XXXX ), which is official documentation proving these accounts aren't mine. 
That was over 30 days ago. More than enough time for them to investigate and fix this. But when I checked my credit report recently, nothing has changed. The unauthorized information is still there, still damaging my credit, still affecting my life. 
This isn't just a delay or an oversight anymore. This is non-compliance with federal law. TransUnion had a legal obligation to investigate my dispute and remove information they couldn't verify. They didn't do it. 
I've done everything right. I followed their process, sent documentation, gave them the legal timeframe. They've done nothing.

Product : Credit Reporting Issue : Inaccurate information ; failure to conduct reasonable investigation ; identity theft ; unlawful reinsertion ; sham investigation practices COMPLAINT NARRATIVE I am filing this complaint against Experian Information Solutions , Inc. for repeated, ongoing, and systemic violations of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), including but not limited to failures under 15 U.S.C. 1681e ( b ), 1681i, 1681c-2, and 1681s-2, resulting in substantial and continuing harm to me as a consumer and as a documented victim of identity theft. 

1. IDENTITY THEFT FORMAL NOTICE PROVIDED AND IGNORED I am a verified victim of identity theft. I have provided Experian with : FTC Identity Theft Report IRS confirmation letter stating that I am a victim of identity theft Ongoing efforts to obtain a police report ( currently in process ) Despite receiving formal identity theft documentation, Experian has failed and refused to block or permanently suppress fraudulent information, as required under FCRA 1681c-2. Experian continues to report accounts that are not mine and that I have never authorized, used, or benefited from.

case_records.narrative:
I am filing a complaint against Equifax Information Services LLC regarding an inadequate and unreasonable reinvestigation of inaccurate credit reporting. 

I disputed a charged-off credit card account on my Equifax credit report due to inconsistent and misleading reporting. Equifax responded that the account was verified, however Equifax simultaneously admits that multiple core data fields were modified during the reinvestigation. 

In Equifaxs dispute results letter, Equifax states that the following fields were modified : additional information, actual payment, date of last payment, date of major delinquency first reported, historical account information, account history, and portfolio status. Equifax also changed the reporting code from CO to L across multiple years. 

Despite these material changes, Equifax still claims the account was reported correctly and provided no explanation for how the information was verified, what documentation was reviewed, or why corrections were necessary if the reporting was accurate. 

Equifaxs response contains internal contradictions, including describing the account as closed at consumer request while also reporting the account as a charged-of

case_records.narrative:
I am a verified victim of identity theft. My consumer credit file contains multiple accounts that I did not open, did not authorize, did not sign for, did not benefit from, and have no factual or legal relationship to whatsoever. These accounts were fraudulently created using my personal identifying information without my knowledge or consent. 

I timely submitted a valid FTC Identity Theft Report along with supporting documentation establishing that these accounts are the direct result of criminal identity theft. Despite receiving legally sufficient proof, you failed to conduct a lawful and reasonable investigation and failed to block and delete the fraudulent information as required by federal law. 

Your continued reporting of these accounts is unlawful. Once an account is identified as identity-theft-related and supported by an FTC Identity Theft Report, the Fair Credit Reporting Act mandates immediate blocking and permanent removal. There is no discretion, no delay, and no justification for continued reporting or verification of fraudulent data. 

Your failure to comply constitutes willful non-compliance with the Fair Credit Reporting Act, including but not limited to violatio

PURPOSE AND OVERVIEW I, XXXX XXXX, a confirmed identity theft victim whose personal in

assessment.why_concerning: HIGH severity: 175 consumers affected by the same pattern at EQUIFAX, INC. and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: automated system, glitch.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: automated adverse actions, decisions given with no explanation, no human reachable.
The pattern repeats across 175 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Automated adverse actions are taken at scale, raising doubt that the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.
Customers cannot reach a human to question or reverse an automated decision — an unreasonable barrier to support and redress.

hypotheses.mechanism: Automated dispute resolution (e-OSCAR-style) is likely closing investigations without genuine human review, leaving errors uncorrected.
hypotheses.evidence: 175 complaints showing automated-investigation and unresolved-dispute cues; concentrated at EQUIFAX, INC., Experian Information Solutions Inc., TRANSUNION INTERMEDIATE HOLDINGS, INC..

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to EQUIFAX, INC. for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask EQUIFAX, INC. directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated decisioning model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

actions.action: Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

sample_narratives:
Company : XXXX / XXXX  Issue : Account frozen / funds withheld Amount : {$50.00} Date problem started : XX/XX/year> ( or the correct year if different ) Complaint : XXXX suspended my account in XXXX for suspicious activity. I completed the required identity verification immediately, but XXXX  has continued to hold my {$50.00} balance for months with no explanation or resolution.

I use this cashapp account for work doing private investigations, as it is an open source resource that I use. I am unable to open another account as the accounts are confirmed with a social security number and if I attempt to open a new one it is flagged and closed.

I am unable to speak directly a supervisor, they can send me an email but I can not reply back or talk to them on the phone, I am unable to speak directly to anyone on the " specialty team '' that is allegedly processing my request and it has now been a month since my account was originally closed. I am getting no resolution fr

CFPB Complaint Venmo Payment Dispute Mishandling I made an XXXX purchase using Venmo as my payment method on XX/XX/2025 in the amount of {$95.00}. I was anxiously waiting for the item to arrive. On XX/XX/2025 at approximately XXXX XXXX, I encountered my USPS XXXX and asked if there was a package for me. He confirmed there was no delivery for my address at that time. Later that day, the shipment status showed the package as delivered around XXXX XXXX  but I never received anything. I contacted USPS and obtained documentation confirming that the package tied to the merchant-provided tracking number was delivered to a different address and not my delivery location. This confirmed that I did not receive the item and that the transaction was fraudulent. I initially filed a dispute with XXXX, submitting the USPS letter as proof. Despite the evidence, XXXX denied my claim. On XX/XX/2025, I contacted Venmo by phone to report the issue and open a dispute. I was told that I would be contacted if additional documentation was needed. No one from Venmo reached out, and I was never given the opportunity to submit the proof I already had. Instead, I received a denial email from Venmo on XX/XX/2025 without any request for supporting evidence.. I have since contacted Venmo multiple times to escalate the case and to provide my XXXX documentation showing misdelivery. Despite this clear proof that I was a victim of mail fraud and did not receive the merchandise. I reached out to them again and appealed with my proof and currently waiting to hear back, but I lost trust in Venmo and need your assistance. I am requesting CFPB assistance to require Venmo to properly investigate this transaction, review the evidence, and refund the payment for the undelivered item.

case_records.product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
case_records.issue: Trouble accessing funds in your mobile or digital wallet
case_records.narrative:
We are a small nonprofit that uses Venmo occasionally for customers or donors who don't have easy access to other means of payment. Venmo has locked our account from accessing funds while it requests identity documents. We have uploaded 2 separate sets of identity documents 4 times : clearly lit, clear photo, clear name and date of birth. Each time Venmo rejects them and will not tell us what's wrong with these documents, only emails us from an " unmonitored '' email inbox. 

When we ask to speak to support : - Venmo 's " Chat With Us '' link sends us to an unhelpful FAQ + an AI chatbot that is unable to provide answers - Venmo 's support line, a phone number, put us on hold for 20 minutes and then abruptly hung up, forcing us to start over. 
- We tried again, and this time chose the " callback '' option with their support line, but Venmo never called us back. 
- XXXXPaypal seems set up in every possible way to avoid letting customers speak to a human and get meaningful feedback or help instead of automated emails or useless AI chatbots. 

We just want access to our account!

The issue is a deceptive business practice regarding custody of funds : Coinbase XXXX claims the assets are " unrecoverable '' or " lost '' due to the network mismatch. However, public blockchain data irrefutably proves that Coinbase has taken active custody of these funds. 

Evidence of XXXX : XXXX : XXXX XXXX was successfully sent to my deposit address ( Address : XXXX ). ( Tx Hash : XXXX ) XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX : Coinbase 's automated system accessed my deposit address and transferred these exact funds OUT to their central corporate hot wallet, identified on blockchain explorers as " Coinbase XXXX '' ( Wallet Address : XXXX ). 

XXXX XXXX XXXX : XXXX Conclusion : Coinbase has exercised ownership rights by sweeping the funds to their internal hot wallet. They are currently in possession of my XXXX USDT. Refusing to credit my account while retaining the funds in their corporate wallet constitutes unjust enrichment

On XX/XX/XXXX, I received a text message from the phone number XXXX ( XXXX ) XXXX stating : CITIBANK : DID YOU SPEND {$470.00} PURCHASE @ XXXX & XXXX. XX/XX/XXXX? Reply YES=1 / NO=2.

I replied 2, indicating the charge was unauthorized. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I received another text message from the same number stating : CITIBANK : DID YOU SPEND {$500.00} PURCHASE @ XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX. XX/XX/XXXX? Reply YES=1 / NO=2.

case_records.narrative:
To Whom It May Concern, I am submitting this message as a formal written complaint and request for escalation regarding an unresolved restriction on my Credit Karma Money account and virtual debit card. 

My account appears to be restricted or placed on hold, preventing me from accessing my virtual debit card and/or transferring funds. When I contacted support via chat, I was informed that for security purposes no information could be provided. No explanation, resolution steps, or timeline were offered. 

While I understand that financial institutions may place temporary restrictions due to security or compliance reviews, I am requesting the following, which I am entitled to as an account holder : 1. Written confirmation of the general category of the restriction ( e.g., identity verification, compliance review, fraud prevention ), without disclosure of sensitive internal triggers.
