I am filing this complaint because Citibank has prevented me from completing an international USD wire transfer from my personal Citibank account to my own XXXX XXXX  XXXX USD account in XXXX, and has failed to provide any effective written support, technical explanation, or formal complaint channel. 

I followed Citibanks own online banking process step by step to register the international payee. Citis platform allowed me to select XXXX, USD, XXXX XXXX  XXXX, and SWIFT XXXX. I entered the XXXX, beneficiary information, bank address, and intermediary bank details. At the final stage, Citibanks system produced a technical error and prevented the payee from being added. I have documented each step of the process with screenshots.

I then tried to solve the issue through multiple Citibank channels : online banking, chatbot, phone support, my account manager, and branch/relationship management. The response was completely ineffective. The chatbot only redirected me to call Citibank. When I spoke with a specialist, I was told there was no error or restriction on my account, even though I have screenshots showing that Citis platform was producing an error and blocking the payee registra

assessment.why_concerning: HIGH severity: 138 consumers affected by the same pattern at Paypal Holdings, Inc and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: algorithm, automated decision, automated system, chatbot.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: actions taken without notice, automated adverse actions, automated loops, decisions given with no explanation.
The pattern repeats across 138 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Accounts/cards are actioned without notice, denying customers the chance to respond before harm crystallises.
Automated adverse actions are taken at scale, raising doubt that the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: Automated AML / sanctions screening is likely freezing withdrawals; a recent model change may have increased false positives on legitimate users.
hypotheses.evidence: 138 complaints showing withdrawal-freeze and verification-failure cues; concentrated at Paypal Holdings, Inc, Block, Inc., BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to Paypal Holdings, Inc for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask Paypal Holdings, Inc directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated monitoring model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

clusters.name: Cards & Payments — Synchrony, Accounts, Closed — Closing your account
sample_narratives:
I am filing this complaint because CareCredit ( Synchrony Bank ) repeatedly lowered my credit limit immediately after large payments, without any adverse action notice and despite my account being in good standing. 

The first time this happened, my credit limit was reduced from XXXX to XXXX directly after I paid the account down. I contacted the company at that time and asked whether this would happen again. I was told it would not. 

Recently, I paid approximately XXXX toward my balance. Immediately after this payment posted, CareCredit lowered my credit limit again, this time down to XXXX. I received no explanation and no adverse action notice. 

These sudden decreases cause my credit report to show nearly XXXX percent utilization even though I am making responsible and significant payments. This misrepresents my true credit standing and has negatively affected my credit score. 

I am requesting an investigation into the repeated credit limit reductions that occur immediately after payments, the lack of legally required adverse action notices, and the inaccurate reporting impact. I would like my previous credit limit restored or the credit reporting corrected.

Synchrony Bank imposed undisclosed deferred-interest charges on my CareCredit account and charged me for a Payment Security program that did not apply to my situation. None of the key terms were ever explained verbally, in writing, or by email. During this period, I was undergoing a XXXX XXXX for a XXXX XXXX in my XXXX making it impossible to evaluate complex terms even if they had been providedbut they were not. The retroactive interest was completely unforeseeable. 

I was told the financing was XXXX year interest free, with standard interest afterward. No one ever disclosed that it was a deferred-interest plan or that failing to pay the exact remaining balance by the expiration date would trigger retroactive interest from the original purchase date. I searched my records and received no disclosures of any kind. I would never have accepted this structure ; I could have used a regular credit card for far less total interest. 

When the promotion expired in XX/XX/year>, Synchrony added {$6000.00} in retroactive interest, causing my balance and minimum payments to spike. This was the first time I learned retroactive interest even existed on this account, and it has continued generating new interest charges each cycle. I was also charged {$160.00} per month for Payment Security ( I believe about {$2600.00} YTD ) without being told that self-employed individuals do not qualify for the programs primary benefit ( balance cancellation ). The coverage I believed I was paying for never applied to me. 

These practicesundisclosed retroactive interest, charging for inapplicable Payment Security, and doing so during medical hardshipcaused severe financial harm. I have now lost my job and am on the edge of bankruptcy. I would never have agreed to this credit had any of these terms been disclosed. 

When I've explained this to them, they simply said it was listed in my monthly statement. However, I had my account set to autopay every month, and there was no reason for me to go b

Synchrony Bank closed my XXXX 's account that had a $ XXXX credit limit and XXXX balance on XX/XX/XXXX. I was not given any warning. I called and have been getting the run around ever since. My XXXX 's card was taken over by Synchrony Bank in XX/XX/XXXX. On XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX they initially lowered my credit limit from {$9000.00} to {$1500.00}, I immediately had it increased to $ XXXX. IT HAD A XXXX BALANCE AND WAS IN GOOD STANDING!!! This wrecked massive damage on my credit score because this was a long-term account and 100 % available amount. Synchrony Bank then lowered the credit limit of my XXXX XXXX card from {$8800.00} to {$4400.00} on XX/XX/XXXX, This caused another massive hit and once again lowered my credit score has my utilization score went from 51 % to 99 %! I have had this card since XXXX. Then on they closed my XXXX XXXX account on XX/XX/XXXX This has caused another massive hit on my utilization score and longevity WHICH THEY HAVE CAUSED!!!!!! 
Synchrony Bank closed my XXXX XXXX XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX that has a {$4000.00} credit limit and a {$600.00} balance. I use this account to pay for any and all car repairs! 
This lowered my credit score again! Again, my credit score took a massive hit as this was also a long-term account. They never contacted me to ask about it, all off these accounts were taken over by Synchrony Bank : I can't even pay these companies directly!!!!! THIS HAS TO STOP!!! I am scared that without warning, they will close my Care Credit which I use for medical and dental care. THEY CAUSED MY CREDIT SCORES TO DECREASE BY CLOSING THES XXXX ACCOUNTS and XXXX excuse given was because I don't have a mortgage. Did they bother to ask why I don't have a mortgage? NO!!!!! It is because I live with my fiance who has the house under a VA LOAN!

case_records.sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card
case_records.narrative:
I am submitting this complaint regarding Synchrony Banks reduction of my credit limit immediately after I utilized my credit line following direct marketing and solicitation from Synchrony. 

Synchrony actively marketed and encouraged me to use my available credit. In reliance on this solicitation, I used the credit line as promoted. Shortly thereafter, Synchrony reduced my credit limit to reflect approximately 90 % utilization, which materially increased my utilization ratio and caused a steep decline in my credit score. 

Synchrony advised that this adverse action was based on an automated algorithm citing the following factors : Bank card balance-to-income ratio Allegedly insufficient payment amounts Loan balances This constitutes an adverse action under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), 15 U.S.C. 1681a ( k ), and triggers specific notice, accuracy, and disclosure obligations.

The stated reasons for the adverse action are inconsistent with my documented credit profile and raise concerns regarding compliance with FCRA 1681e ( b ) and 1681m ( a ) : My credit report reflects 100 % on-time payments across all credit accounts All payments on the Synchrony account were made on

assessment.why_concerning: MEDIUM severity: 44 consumers affected by the same pattern at SYNCHRONY FINANCIAL and others, and it is accelerating fast (+100% in 7 days). Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: algorithm, automated decision, automated system, computer error.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: automated loops, decisions given with no explanation, no human reachable.
The pattern repeats across 44 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Customers are trapped in automated loops with no escalation path, frustrating their ability to resolve the issue.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: An automated account-closure / fraud model appears to be exiting customers and reversing payments without manual review or notice.
hypotheses.evidence: 44 complaints showing automated closure and payment-reversal cues; concentrated at SYNCHRONY FINANCIAL, CITIBANK, N.A., Bread Financial Holdings, Inc..

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to SYNCHRONY FINANCIAL for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask SYNCHRONY FINANCIAL directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated monitoring model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

Company : Wells Fargo Bank Issue Type : Checking or savings account Problem : Problem with a deposit or withdrawal Sub-issue : ATM deposit not credited On XX/XX/XXXX, I deposited cash into a Wells Fargo ATM. The ATM accepted the deposit ; however, {$100.00} of that deposit was never credited to my account. 
I promptly filed a claim to avoid any delay in reporting. Wells Fargo later denied my claim based solely on their statement that the ATM audit did not reveal any loose or jammed funds. 

I respectfully dispute this conclusion. The absence of loose cash during an audit does not conclusively prove that the ATM did not malfunction or temporarily retain the funds. Below is a clear and accurate account of what occurred. 

Account Compromise and Initial Fraud Before any phone calls, my Wells Fargo online banking, checking account, credit cards, and my childs savings account were accessed without my authorization. I was locked out of my account while unauthorized transfers were being made internally. 

I then received calls from spoofed Wells Fargo numbers. The callers used real Wells Fargo phone numbers, scripts, and terminology. They claimed to be Wells Fargo Fraud and told me my local branch was under investigation. They walked me through a password reset and told me to secure my money before the fraudsters do. 

My Attempt to Protect My Funds I moved my mon

However, the receiving Wells Fargo account had already been closed prior to the deposit attempt. Wells Fargos own mobile banking application clearly identifies the account as closed and unavailable for transactions. Despite this, the refund was still marked as issued, and there has been no clear explanation regarding whether the funds were rejected, returned, or are being held internally by the bank. 


XXXX XXXX advised me to contact Wells Fargo directly regarding the status of the funds and the return process. I contacted the bank seeking clarification and assistance regarding : Whether the ACH deposit was automatically rejected due to the account closure ; Whether the funds are being held internally despite the account no longer existing ; When the funds will be returned to XXXX XXXX so the refund can be reissued correctly ; and Why there ha

assessment.why_concerning: MEDIUM severity: 22 consumers affected by the same pattern at WELLS FARGO & COMPANY, and it is accelerating fast (+100% in 7 days). Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: algorithm, automated decision, automated system, glitch.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: actions taken without notice, automated adverse actions, automated loops, decisions given with no explanation.
The pattern repeats across 22 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Accounts/cards are actioned without notice, denying customers the chance to respond before harm crystallises.
Automated adverse actions are taken at scale, raising doubt that the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: The firm likely tightened an automated transaction-monitoring / financial-crime model, raising false-positive exit rates — legitimate customers are 'de-risked' and offboarded without manual review.
hypotheses.evidence: 22 complaints showing account-closure and suspicious-activity language concentrated at a few firms; concentrated at WELLS FARGO & COMPANY.

hypotheses.mechanism: An automated fraud-detection model may be flagging legitimate transactions, freezing funds before any human check.
hypotheses.evidence: 22 complaints showing fund-freeze and access-loss cues; concentrated at WELLS FARGO & COMPANY.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to WELLS FARGO & COMPANY for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask WELLS FARGO & COMPANY directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated monitoring model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

clusters.name: Cards & Payments — Fargo, Wells, Wells Fargo — Closing your account
sample_narratives:
Wells Fargo Executive Resolutions Team, Im writing to request immediate assistance regarding a series of unresolved issues tied to an account I have been attempting to close since XX/XX/year>. 
Despite my repeated cancellation requests and one previously filed complaint, the following events have continued occurring : Unsolicited debit cards have been mailed to me multiple timeseven after the account was supposed to be fully closed. 
An unauthorized address, located in a state I have never lived in or used, was added to my account. To date, no one at Wells Fargo has been able to explain how or when this occurred. 
My wife recently received a denial letter for a credit card application she never submitted, raising additional concerns about account security and internal controls. 
Each time I inquire, I am told that Wells Fargo needs more time, yet no meaningful updates or resolutions have been provided. 
At this point, the situation has escalated beyond a routine service issue. These are clear indicators of potential system errors, unauthorized account activity, or possible identity-related breaches occurring within your processes. 
Im requesting the following : A full written explanation of how these events occurred, including the unauthorized address change and unsolicited card reissues.

Immediate confirmation of the permanent closure of all accounts associated with my profile. 
A complete audit of all activity tied to my name and my wifes name from XX/XX/year> to present. 
Written verification that no credit products were opened, attempted, or processed without explicit authorization. 
A direct point of contact within the Executive Office who can provide timely updates and oversight until this matter is resolved.

My goal is simple : clarity, closure, and confidence that our information is secure. Ive tried resolving this through standard channels for several months, and the lack of answers has made escalation necessary. 
Please confirm receipt of this message an

I went to pay off my Wells Fargo XXXX card and accidentally paid it off with the closed bank account, I believe I did it twice but didnt realize the first time. The second time i realized right away and when I did I called the customer service they said it was fine it will be recredited to me and when it was I paid it off. Wells Fargo still closed my account with no explanation and when I called in they said there was nothing I could do and I would be losing all the points I built up and lose the card even though I called in to XXXX and told them I had made a mistake right when it happened.

* Not a single agent took ownership of the problem. 
* I was given the " runaround, '' with each representative passing the buck to another department without offering a solution. 
* It is clear that due to the ending partnership with XXXX, Wells Fargo support staff are neglecting these accounts and refusing to assist customers. 
Desired Resolution : I am requesting the following immediate actions : * Full Refund : Immediate credit of the {$36.00} interest charge and any other associated " returned payment '' fees. 
* Account Correction : Ensure this administrative error does not negatively impact my credit report or payment history. 
I have been a responsible customer, but I refuse to pay fees generated by Wells Fargo 's technical failures and internal dis

case_records.issue: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
case_records.narrative:
On Tuesday, XX/XX/year>, I contacted Wells Fargo immediately to report and freeze {$9200.00} in pending fraudulent XXXX XXXX XXXX ' transactions. The frontline representative closed the card ending in XXXX but failed to log the dispute correctly. 

Due to an internal Wells Fargo software mapping error, the bank allowed the explicit {$9200.00} in fraudulent charges to seamlessly transfer over and permanently post onto my newly issued credit card number rather than blocking them. Because their automated system checked the old card number and saw that the charges hadn't finalized on that specific line, it erroneously generated a letter dated XX/XX/year> ( Claim ID : XXXX ), stating the transactions 'did not post ' and falsely claiming all posted charges on my account were valid and authorized. 

When I called to correct this administrative error, the bank refused to issue an immediate provisional credit. Instead, they forced me to issue a third credit card number, start a brand-new dispute file, and wait an additional 7 to 10 business days while holding a {$9200.00} fraudulent balance against me on Wells Fargo Case Number XXXX.

assessment.why_concerning: MEDIUM severity: 15 consumers affected by the same pattern at WELLS FARGO & COMPANY, and it is accelerating fast (+100% in 7 days). Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: algorithm, automated system, glitch.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: automated adverse actions, automated loops, decisions given with no explanation, no human reachable.
The pattern repeats across 15 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Automated adverse actions are taken at scale, raising doubt that the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes.
Customers are trapped in automated loops with no escalation path, frustrating their ability to resolve the issue.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: An automated account-closure / fraud model appears to be exiting customers and reversing payments without manual review or notice.
hypotheses.evidence: 15 complaints showing automated closure and payment-reversal cues; concentrated at WELLS FARGO & COMPANY.

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to WELLS FARGO & COMPANY for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask WELLS FARGO & COMPANY directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated monitoring model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

actions.action: Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

clusters.name: Credit Reporting — Experian, Phone, Year — Incorrect information on your report
sample_narratives:
My name is XXXX XXXX, and I reside at XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX, GA XXXX. 

My family and I are long-standing Bank of America XXXX XXXX We maintain significant monthly spendingtypically {$8000.00} to {$12000.00} per monthon our Bank of America XXXX XXXX, which has always been paid in full and on time. We value our relationship with Bank of America and have had a perfect payment history for over ten years. 

We also maintain a secondary account, the XXXX XXXX XXXX Bank of America Credit Card, which we do not actively use. In XX/XX/year>, a single erroneous charge from XXXX in the amount of {$26.00} was posted to this unused account. XXXX later issued a refund, but it was applied as store credit rather than a reversal to the credit card. 

Compounding the issue, Bank of America had our address incorrectly listed as XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX, GA XXXX instead of XXXX XXXX. As a result, we never received a single statement for this account and had no reason to expect any activity on it. 

Because we did not receive the statements and the card is one we never use, the {$26.00} charge unintentionally went past due. When we discovered the error, we immediately paid the balance in full. 

Despite the extremely small dollar amount, Bank of America reported the account as 60 days past due, which caused my credit score to drop by nearly XXXX points. This is the only late payment ever reported on my credit history in the past decade. 

I must also note that my family is legally XXXX, and we repeatedly requested large-print statements for all of our Bank of America accounts XXXX To date, Bank of America has never fulfilled this accommodation request, which contributed to us not being aware of the issue. 

We have contacted Bank of America more than ten times, requesting removal of this highly disproportionate late payment notation. Given our long history of excellent credit management, the minimal amount involved, and the fact that the late payment resulted from both incorrect address

Description of complaint : I have had XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX accounts over the last 15 years and my payments have been outstanding for every single account! Recently, I received an inaccurate report on my credit bureau report - all three ( Equifax, XXXX, and XXXX  ) stating my past account ( recently paid off by the dealer ) - prior to my new account ( XXXX. 2025 ) - has been paid late. 
I'm SPEECHLESS! My accounts have NEVER been PAID LATE and apparently, there is a glitch ( SOMEWHERE ) that is now affecting my credit - and no one seems to know how to fix it! So as much as it pains me to file a CFPB complaint because I have always had a good relationship with XXXX XXXX - this must be done to apparently correct this issue- an issue that is causing " my '' credit score to drop - an issue that I DID NOT CAUSE OR HAVE ABSOLUTELY NOTHING TO DO WITH!

In late XX/XX/year>, I notified XXXX that our household was impacted by the federal government shutdown and that my income had been interrupted. I was told that my accounts would be updated under the shutdown hardship accommodation. 

On XX/XX/year>, I submitted the required hardship documentation through the secure message portal. The portal states that I would receive a response within one business day. As of today, both messages remain unread, and no action has been taken.

Despite being under a verified shutdown hardship, XXXX reported my account as delinquent, lowered my credit limit, and applied adverse credit reporting to my XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  ( ending in XXXX ). These negative actions occurred after I submitted the hardship documentation and requested assistance. 

The adverse reporting conflicts with federal protections related to hardship accommodations. I attempted multiple times to resolve this by phone, but no one reviewed my submitted documents, and no accommodation was applied. 

These errors have caused negative impacts to my credit score and unnecessary financial hardship, despite my timely request for relief.

These errors have caused negative impacts to my credit score and unnecessary financial hardship, despite my timely request for relief.

case_records.product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
case_records.narrative:
Experian is reporting inaccurate and unverifiable information on my credit report, including a bankruptcy public record and multiple old/paid accounts that Experian claims they verified, although no verification took place with the original source.

I disputed these items through Experians system. Instead of conducting a real investigation, Experian relied on an automated system to verify the items without contacting the original source of the information. Bankruptcy courts do NOT report information directly to Experian, yet Experian claims a third party certified the information. The furnisher of the information was not identified.

Under FCRA 611 ( a ) ( 7 ), I requested the Method of Verification. Experian refused to provide : The name of the furnisher The address and phone The date the item was verified The procedure used to verify the disputed information Experian simply responded that the item remains, which is not a valid response and does not satisfy federal law.

case_records.product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
case_records.issue: Problem with a company's investigation into an existing problem
case_records.narrative:
I am filing this complaint because Experian violated multiple sections of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) during my dispute process. Experian refused to provide required information, misrepresented my rights, and admitted to using an automated system instead of performing a lawful investigation. 

Here is what happened : 1. Experian refused to provide the Method of Verification ( MOV ) After Experian marked an account as verified, I requested the Method of Verification under FCRA 611 ( a ) ( 6 ). 
The Experian representative told me : We dont do MOV. 

This is false and violates FCRA, which requires Experian to provide : The name of the person they contacted at the furnisher Their job/title The procedure used to verify the information The business name, address, and phone number Experian refused all of this. 

2. Experian admitted they did NOT conduct a reasonable investigation The representative stated : We just go off what the furnisher has. 

This means Experian did not : independently investigate review documentation verify evidence speak to anyone confirm accuracy They simply accepted whatever the furnisher provided. 
Courts have ruled repeatedly that this violates FCRA

case_records.product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
case_records.narrative:
On XX/XX/year>, Experian completed a reinvestigation of a tradeline furnished by XXXX XXXX Bank, XXXX. ( XXXX XXXX ) and deleted the account from my Experian credit file. This deletion was confirmed in Experians dispute results. 

On or about XX/XX/year>, Experian reinserted the same XXXX XXXX tradeline into my credit file. The tradeline currently appears only on Experian and does not appear on XXXX  or XXXX. 

Experian did not provide any written notice of reinsertion within five ( 5 ) business days, nor did Experian provide proof of certification of accuracy prior to reinsertion, as required by 15 U.S.C. 1681i ( a ) ( 5 ) ( B ). 

To date, I have received no reinsertion notice, no certification documentation, and no explanation justifying the reinsertion. 

This constitutes a clear violation of the Fair Credit Reporting Act.

Harm Caused Improper reporting on one bureau only Damage to my credit profile and accuracy Loss of statutory consumer protections Time and expense required to address non-compliance

I have made several attempts to resolve these inaccuracies directly with the companies that furnished the incorrect information to your agency. I contacted each creditor and collection agency listed and spoke with the representatives assigned to my file ; however, despite explaining the errors in detail, no corrective action was taken. I was repeatedly assured that updates would be submitted to the credit reporting agencies, but those updates were never made, and the inaccurate information continues to be reported. 

In addition to these conversations, I made multiple follow-up calls to ensure the matter was being addressed. Unfortunately, many of these calls resulted in excessively long hold times, and on several occasions, the automated system disconnected my call before I could reach a representative. These repeated attempts demonstrate my good-faith effort to resolve the matter directly with the data furnishers, yet the inaccuracies remain uncorrected. For these reasons, I am now formally requesting your assistance under the Fair Credit Reporting Act to investigate and remove all unverified or inaccurate information from my credit file.

case_records.narrative: On XXXX XX/XX/year>, I applied for a new credit card, and was told that Experian had my account frozen. In checking with the company, I have found them unable to respond by voice to me : all their phone response trees seem to end only in referrals to their website. In trying that remedy, I could not log in, and there is no one to help with tech support to help that process. I was finally able, on XXXX XX/XX/year>, to speak to someone who told me that my account was unfrozen but locked, and that I needed to log in to unlock the profile online. But since I can not seem to log in, I can't unlock the profile. So, I feel stuck and with no place to turn. It is frustrating that I can not speak to a " specialist '' or any XXXX, and I now worry that I can not open any credit account or work with my financial status with my profile locked with no apparent way to unlock it.

case_records.product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
case_records.issue: Problem with fraud alerts or security freezes
case_records.narrative:
This is in response to the Credit Reporting business Experian and my interactions with them online on XX/XX/year> and online/calling on XX/XX/year>. 

I was recommended by other parties to set up a freeze on my credit reporting after another party tried to use my identify taking out a loan.

I attempted to sign up for an account online but their system either reports technical difficulties off and on for the past two days or informs me it can not confirm my identity and that I have to call or write to complete the process.

When I call, the automated system indicates it too has a problem but will not forward me on to a representative. Their writing method informed me I needed to send copies of identification documentation, which is not recommended from what I've learned.

Thus their refusal to allow incoming calls to access live operators leaves people that need to use their services in an impossible situation and more vulnerable such as is my case. 

Thank you.

case_records.narrative:
Several years ago I was involved in the XXXX  security breach which left me very concerned about credit reporting agencies. More recently I received notification from XXXX  earlier this year that my data had been breached and set up a monitoring serivce with My XXXX  that they offered. A few days ago, I received an e-mail from Experian but moved into a folder as I did not have time to review it. I just opened to review and it reflects that it was received today. As I was scrolling through the text, I accidentially clicked on a link XXXX that indicated it as suspicious e-mail. I reached out to Experian for an explanation but unable to reach a live person to discuss. I am attaching the e-mail received. Today, at approximately XXXX, I received an e-mail notification from My TrueIdentity that I had a critical change to my credit report which I froze with all three credit reporting agencies following the Equifax breach. I no longer know what to believe is real or not and unable to get to a live person to discuss. 

After last complaint against Experian, I received a phone message from an unidenfied number but would not input any verification information as I did not know if it was legit

case_records.issue: Problem with a company's investigation into an existing problem
case_records.narrative:
My information was hacked. Someone in a different state opened a credit card with. XXXX XXXX using my information. I have been disputing this since it showed up on my credit report in XXXX. This is a third party that contracts with banks to approve cards. The card was approved through XXXX XXXX XXXX I sent in documents proving I don't live where the card was sent to. I had to resend it because the company failed to change their address. I never received anything saying that they received it. ( I think they sent it to the fraud address ) I finally found a phone number for XXXX XXXX. As proof this is not my card, the automated system did not recognize my phone number or zip code. To talk to a person I had to say it was stolen. 
This was around 2 weeks ago and I still haven't seen confirmation in writing like I asked that this is not my account. I have since filled an FTC complaint as well as a police report. All of these were submitted by email to XXXX XXXX. And feels unnecessary because it was a small credit limit. 
I expected a more timely resolution. To me, this company has very shady practices and I've been questioning it's legitimate since my first letter was returned.

XXXX. XXXX must provide a formal " Notice of Wrongful Dishonor '' specifically for XXXX XXXX XXXX, admitting the payment failure was a result of a bank freeze on verified funds.

case_records.product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
case_records.issue: Problem with fraud alerts or security freezes
case_records.narrative: Experian has again put a security freeze on my credit without my permission. I have had no issues since encountering the same problem back in XXXX to XXXX. When I file a complaint they come back with I will hear in 60 days. Thats unacceptable. I sent them a certified letter with my drivers license name change social and address. This is illegal and I am not waiting 60 days to have a bogus security freeze removed. I have had no issues for the past few years until now. I am not waiting 60 days. I dont have security freeze on my other credit bureaus. I need my credit report and security freezes removed. My employer can not pull my reports because of this. When you illegally interfere with someones job its a serious issue. I was also locked out of my account with you so I created an account no one can get in. I want Experian to unlock my credit. Again its illegal for them to do this. I cant join their membership nothing because of this bogus freeze and I am not waiting 60 days to become a member and get my credit reports. All documentation has been sent to them twice.

case_records.product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
case_records.narrative:
Your choices dont match my complaint. This is about the credit reporting company, not about misinformation received by it. 

Yesterday and today XX/XX/year> I tried to delete/close my account with Experian. After signing in and following all directions on their website including their Support Help, XXXX chat bot, and phone number given by their chat bot I am unable to locate how to close my account. The chat bot explained they didnt have the information to do that, and I had to call their automated phone system as no email or phone number were available to converse with a real person. The automated phone system said they didnt have the information and I had to do it online at XXXX, which is where I started. The chat bot didnt have an answer about how to escalate this request and offered only escalation information to complain about other companies misinformation about me.

case_records.product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
case_records.narrative:
My vehicle financed through Wells Fargo Auto was declared a total loss on or around [ XX/XX/year> ], and my insurance company is currently processing the claim and payoff. 

Despite this, I was charged for a payment and Wells Fargo reported the account as having a missed payment, which has negatively impacted my credit. 

I have made multiple attempts to contact Wells Fargo Auto by phone to resolve this issue and request information regarding my account status and how payments are being handled during the total loss process. I have been unable to reach a representative or receive assistance. 

This missed payment is inaccurate, as the account should be handled appropriately during an active insurance claim. 

I am requesting that Wells Fargo Auto : Correct the inaccurate credit reporting Remove the missed payment from my credit report Review and properly update my account to reflect total loss status Ensure no further negative reporting occurs during this process

assessment.why_concerning: MEDIUM severity: 116 consumers affected by the same pattern at Experian Information Solutions Inc. and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: algorithm, automated system, chatbot, glitch.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: actions taken without notice, automated loops, decisions given with no explanation, no human reachable.
The pattern repeats across 116 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Accounts/cards are actioned without notice, denying customers the chance to respond before harm crystallises.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: Automated dispute resolution (e-OSCAR-style) is likely closing investigations without genuine human review, leaving errors uncorrected.
hypotheses.evidence: 116 complaints showing automated-investigation and unresolved-dispute cues; concentrated at Experian Information Solutions Inc., TRANSUNION INTERMEDIATE HOLDINGS, INC., EQUIFAX, INC..

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to Experian Information Solutions Inc. for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask Experian Information Solutions Inc. directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated decisioning model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

actions.action: Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

clusters.name: Other — Problem with customer service
sample_narratives:
To Whom It May Concern, I am writing to formally dispute the inaccurate and improperly reported bankruptcy information appearing on the credit file of XXXX XXXX. This letter serves as a notice of dispute, a demand for strict compliance with federal law, and a request for immediate corrective action.

Pursuant to the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), specifically 15 U.S.C. 1681b ( a ) ( 2 ), a consumer reporting agency may only furnish or report information when there is a lawful and verifiable permissible purpose. You are hereby placed on notice that the bankruptcy information you are reporting lacks demonstrated permissible purpose and has not been properly verified as required by law.

Further, under 15 U.S.C. 1681q, any person who knowingly and willfully obtains or furnishes consumer information under false pretenses is subject to federal liability. Continued reporting of unverified or inaccurately attributed bankruptcy data may constitute reporting under false pretenses if proper authorization and verification can not be produced.

Additionally, pursuant to 18 U.S.C. 1028A, strict identity verification is required where information may be the result of misidentification or identity-related error. I dispute that this bankruptcy has been accurately matched to me, and I demand proof that the information reported has been verified through reliable and lawful means.

Under 15 U.S.C. 1681c-2, I am exercising my right to block the reporting of inaccurate information resulting from identity error or misattribution. You are required to block and remove this bankruptcy entry unless and until you obtain direct, documentary verification from the courthouse of record, confirming : My full legal name, XXXX Social Security number ( full or last XXXX digits ), My date of birth, and A certified match confirming I am the subject of the alleged bankruptcy.

clusters.name: Banking — Check, Funds, Deposit — Managing an account
sample_narratives:
Subject : Urgent : Funds Withheld Over 30 Days Immediate Release or Return Required To Whom It May Concern, I am writing to request immediate assistance regarding a serious issue involving a {$3000.00} check I wrote from my XXXX XXXX XXXX  account. The intended payee deposited the check through Bank of America mobile deposit. Bank of America withdrew the full {$3000.00} from my account ; however, the funds have not been released to the payee nor returned to my bank.

It Has Been Over 30 Days With No Resolution This issue has now been ongoing for more than 30 days.

During this time : Bank of America has not released the funds, Bank of America has not returned the funds, and No written or verbal explanation has been provided regarding the prolonged hold.

A delay exceeding 30 days is unacceptable and inconsistent with normal banking regulations and check processing procedures, especially since the funds were fully withdrawn from my account.

Summary of the Issue Check amount : {$3000.00} Deposited via Bank of America mobile deposit Funds were withdrawn from my XXXX XXXX account immediately Both banks acknowledge that the money was taken by Bank of America Bank of America is holding the funds without explanation Repeated attempts to resolve this with Bank of America have resulted in being transferred between departments, receiving conflicting answers, or being told no one can help because the deposit was mobile. There has been zero progress.

Summary : USAA Federal Savings Bank incorrectly altered the amount of a mobile check deposit from {$1500.00} to {$15.00} three days after the funds were credited to my account. USAAs automated system made this change without human review, without notifying me, and deducted {$1400.00} from my account. When contacted about the error, the representative confirmed that the check images in their system clearly stated {$1500.00} but also stated that there was absolutely no way to correct the error and that the only way for me to get the money back was to ask the original check issuer to write me a new check for the difference. I escalated the request to their executive branch, and I received an email reiterating the original representative 's statements. 

Issue : The original check is clearly written for {$1500.00}. Despite acknowledging the error, USAA representatives and supervisors stated they can not correct the error and that only the check issuer can remedy the situation. This is an improper denial of responsibility for an entirely internal banking error. 

Impact : This caused my account balance to be incorrect without notification or consent.

This email serves as formal notice of Bank of Americas deceptive practices, predatory account-retention mechanisms, and deliberate obstruction of lawful account closure. What follows is not a customer service complaint. It is a record of systemic misconduct. 
Bank of Americas rewards system displayed a balance of {$390.00} associated with my Visa Signature card. That figure was presented as real, available value. Any reasonable consumer would understand this to represent transferable funds. Acting on that representation, I opened a checking account for the sole purpose of accessing what Bank of Americas own interface indicated I possessed. 
Only after the account was opened did the truth emerge : the {$390.00} figure was illusory. The actual balance was XXXX reward points, effectively worthless. This is not a misunderstanding. It is a deceptive presentation of value embedded in your systems. Had Bank of America accurately represented the nature of those rewards, the checking account would never have been opened. 
What followed escalates this from deception to coercion. 
Bank of America has constructed its systems so that a checking account can not be closed through any normal, mode

assessment.why_concerning: MEDIUM severity: 60 consumers affected by the same pattern at BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: algorithm, artificial intelligence, automated loop, automated system.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: actions taken without notice, automated adverse actions, automated loops, decisions given with no explanation.
The pattern repeats across 60 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Accounts/cards are actioned without notice, denying customers the chance to respond before harm crystallises.
Automated adverse actions are taken at scale, raising doubt that the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: The firm likely tightened an automated transaction-monitoring / financial-crime model, raising false-positive exit rates — legitimate customers are 'de-risked' and offboarded without manual review.
hypotheses.evidence: 60 complaints showing account-closure and suspicious-activity language concentrated at a few firms; concentrated at BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, CITIBANK, N.A., UNITED SERVICES AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION.

hypotheses.mechanism: An automated fraud-detection model may be flagging legitimate transactions, freezing funds before any human check.
hypotheses.evidence: 60 complaints showing fund-freeze and access-loss cues; concentrated at BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, CITIBANK, N.A., UNITED SERVICES AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION.

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated monitoring model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

actions.action: Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

'' These statements are fundamentally contradictory and demonstrate that Chase has misled both me and the CFPB about their actual review capabilities and processes.I clearly stated that my current monthly debt obligations are significantly lower than what appears on credit reports : XXXX validation

Chase sent me an automated fraud alert for an unfamiliar {$5.00} charge. I responded No, expecting the bank to verify the transaction. Instead, Chase immediately shut down my debit card without providing any notice that the card was being deactivated. I learned of the shutdown only after multiple legitimate recurring payments began failing.

Why this is a violation : Chases actions appear to violate several consumer-protection standards : Regulation E ( 12 CFR 1005 ) requires that banks provide timely notice when restricting access to a consumers debit card or electronic fund transfers. Chase did not notify me in any way that the card was being closed.

This is potentially an unfair practice under UDAAP because Chases automated decision caused significant and predictable harm ( failed payments, fees, service interruptions ) while offering no reasonable consumer benefit and providing no opportunity for me to correct or verify the alert.

The card closure was disproportionate to the situationone low-dollar charge with no fraud patternshowing a systemically unreasonable fraud-workflow design that harms consumers.

No adverse action notice was provided, despite restricting my access to an active payment instrument linked to multiple accounts.

Chase failed to follow reasonable verification procedures before taking action that impacted every account linked to that debit card.

Resulting damages : Because Chase closed the card without notice, several legitimate payments were declined, causing financial loss, service interruptions, and the risk of negative credit reporting. Chases conduct deprived me of access to an active and relied-upon payment method with no opportunity to prevent the harm.

What I am requesting : The CFPB should escalate this matter to Chase and require the bank to : Provide a written explanation of their justification for closing a debit card without any notice after a routine fraud-alert response.

Explain how this practice complies with Regulation E

case_records.issue: Advertising and marketing, including promotional offers
case_records.narrative:
I am submitting this complaint regarding approximately {$2000.00} in interest charges assessed on my Chase business credit card, which resulted from a good-faith misunderstanding during the application process and Chases refusal to provide a meaningful manual review or accommodation. 

In late XX/XX/year>, I applied for a Chase business credit card believing I was selecting a product offering a 0 % introductory APR for 15 months, which was actively advertised at the time. I accessed the application through a Chase-provided promotional link and proceeded under the clear impression that I was accepting the 0 % introductory APR offer . Based on that understanding, I allowed a balance to carry. Had I known the card carried a 19.99 % variable APR, I would not have done so. 

Unbeknownst to me, I was routed to or selected a different Chase business credit card that did not include the 0 % introductory APR promotion. I only became aware of this months later when a Chase branch representative reviewed my account and informed me that the card I opened did not match the advertised promotional XXXX. At that point, I reviewed my statements and discovered that substantial interest had accrued.

case_records.sub_product: General-purpose credit card or charge card
case_records.narrative:
I am filing this complaint regarding the sudden closure of my American Express credit card accounts and the forfeiture of my accumulated Membership Rewards points without explanation. 

My accounts were closed without prior notice following what American Express described as a routine review. When I contacted the company for clarification, I was informed only that American Express reserves the right to close accounts at its discretion under the Cardmember Agreement. No specific reason or evidence of wrongdoing was provided. 

At the time the accounts were closed, they were in good standing and all balances were paid according to the terms of the account. Over time I had accumulated a substantial number of Membership Rewards points through legitimate spending in accordance with the program rules. 

Upon closing my accounts, American Express immediately forfeited these earned rewards and informed me that the points could not be redeemed. 

The loss of rewards earned through legitimate purchases and program participation has caused financial harm and raises concerns regarding fairness and transparency in how American Express administers its Membership Rewards program.

assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 39 consumers affected by the same pattern at JPMORGAN CHASE & CO. and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: algorithm, automated decision, automated loop, automated system.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: actions taken without notice, decisions given with no explanation, no human reachable.
The pattern repeats across 39 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Accounts/cards are actioned without notice, denying customers the chance to respond before harm crystallises.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: An automated account-closure / fraud model appears to be exiting customers and reversing payments without manual review or notice.
hypotheses.evidence: 39 complaints showing automated closure and payment-reversal cues; concentrated at JPMORGAN CHASE & CO., BARCLAYS BANK DELAWARE, CAPITAL ONE FINANCIAL CORPORATION.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to JPMORGAN CHASE & CO. for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask JPMORGAN CHASE & CO. directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated monitoring model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

clusters.name: Payments & Crypto — Paypal, Automated, Transaction — Fraud or scam
sample_narratives:
In XXXX, my PayPal account was compromised and used to make a series of unauthorized charges, including a {$610.00} transaction that PayPal is still trying to hold me responsible for. 

The hijacking of my account was flagrant and obvious. Someone gained access, changed email and physical addresses on my PayPal account, and immediately began attempting to siphon funds. I did not make or authorize these transactions, and I did not give anyone else permission to use my account. 

As soon as I became aware of the activity, I notified PayPal that my account had been hacked and that these charges were unauthorized. In a written communication on XX/XX/XXXX, I told PayPal very clearly : I did not make these transactions. 
I did not authorize them. 
Someone fraudulently charged to my account. 
Its plain to see someone added different email addresses and physical addresses to my account in order to commit fraud. 
Do not process ANY more payments. No debits to my bank or credit card. Please cease. 

Despite this explicit notice and request that no further payments be processed, PayPal continued processing the disputed activity. XXXX of the key transactions is a {$610.00} instant transfer payment to XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX, which my bank ( XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX declined when PayPal attempted to pull funds from my account. 

I spoke with multiple PayPal representatives by phone at the time and was repeatedly assured that I would not be held liable for charges I did not authorize. I relied on these assurances and believed PayPal would protect me from the fraudulent activity on my account. 

Several charges in this same sequence were eventually refunded as fraudulent. However, PayPal has taken an inconsistent and unexplained position by selectively deciding that XXXX charge for {$610.00} is somehow authorized, even though I reported all of the related transactions as unauthorized at the same time and they clearly form a single pattern of account compromise and fraud. 

PayPal has to

I am filing a complaint regarding XXXX  dispute resolution process for Transaction ID XXXX in the amount of {$210.00}. 

PayPal reversed this transaction in favor of the buyer despite my submission of timestamped video evidence demonstrating delivery of the purchased service. The transaction was for participation in a time-specific digital service ( a live online event ), not for a guaranteed physical good. The service was delivered in full. 

XXXX decision appears to have been made without meaningful consideration of the evidence provided and without transparency into how digital services are evaluated when no physical shipment is involved. I was not given a clear explanation of why the evidence was deemed insufficient, nor an opportunity to clarify platform limitations that prevent automated attendance logs. 

I am requesting that PayPal conduct a manual compliance review of this dispute, provide a written explanation of the decision criteria applied, and clarify how sellers of legitimate digital services are protected under XXXX policies. 

This complaint is submitted to request regulatory review of XXXX dispute handling practices and automated decision-making related to digital services.

Issue : PayPal permanently limited my account on XX/XX/XXXX immediately after I received a legitimate payment of {$8100.00}. The limitation was automatic and occurred without any review or explanation. 

What Happened : I received a standard goods and services payment from a verified buyer. The transaction was legitimate, and I shipped to the buyers verified address. My account had no disputes, no violations, and no prior issues. Immediately when the payment arrived, my account was permanently closed without giving me the opportunity to verify my identity or provide documentation. I was not told what policy I supposedly violated. 

Why This Is a Problem : PayPal is holding {$7900.00} of my funds for 180 days without justification. I complied fully with their policy and used PayPal in good faith. Freezing a large amount of money without explanation or review is unfair, and I am being punished for a payment I lawfully received. XXXX automated system appears to have banned me without a human review. 

The email on the account is XXXX XXXX

case_records.issue: Money was not available when promised
case_records.narrative:
On XX/XX/year>, my business processed a single transaction of {$2000.00} for the final delivery of a custom-built software application. Shortly after, I received an automated email from PayPal informing me that my account had been permanently deactivated. The email explicitly stated my account was placed in the 'High Tier ' and subject to a 'High Volume Dispute fee ' due to customer dissatisfaction.

However, this stated reason is mathematically impossible and factually incorrect. My account has exa

ctly one transaction in its entire history, and there are absolutely zero disputes, claims, or chargebacks associated with it.

I contacted PayPal customer service by phone to appeal this algorithmic error. Still, the representative informed me that the automated system had locked their interface and that they lacked authorization to request a manual review or escalate the issue. As a result, PayPal is currently holding my {$2000.00} balance for up to 120 days on the basis of a completely false premise. The system has misidentified a single, fully accepted, and undisputed XXXX software transaction as a high-volume dispute risk, and the company has completely blocked standard customer s

assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 43 consumers affected by the same pattern at Paypal Holdings, Inc, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: algorithm, automated decision, automated loop, automated system.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: automated loops, decisions given with no explanation, no human reachable.
The pattern repeats across 43 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Customers are trapped in automated loops with no escalation path, frustrating their ability to resolve the issue.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: Automated AML / sanctions screening is likely freezing withdrawals; a recent model change may have increased false positives on legitimate users.
hypotheses.evidence: 43 complaints showing withdrawal-freeze and verification-failure cues; concentrated at Paypal Holdings, Inc.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to Paypal Holdings, Inc for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask Paypal Holdings, Inc directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated monitoring model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

sample_narratives:
To Whom It May Concern at the CFPB, I am submitting this complaint regarding Cash Apps failure to resolve a fraudulent transaction I reported. 

On XX/XX/year>, I filed a dispute with Cash App concerning a fraudulent transaction. 
As of XX/XX/year>, it has been 94 days since my initial complaint, far exceeding the XXXX-day investigation period Cash App advised. 
Despite repeated emails and chats with Cash App XXXX, I have only been told the matter was escalated, with no clear explanation of the investigation or progress. 


Most concerning is the inconsistency in Cash Apps handling of the case : Cash App refunded {$13.00} of the fraudulent transaction. 
However, the remaining {$660.00} has not been refunded, despite being part of the same fraudulent transaction. 
Cash App has provided no explanation for why XXXX portion was refunded while the other remains unresolved. 


This prolonged delay, lack of transparency, and inconsistent resolution are unacceptable. I have provided all necessary information multiple times, yet Cash App continues to stall without justification. 

I respectfully request that the CFPB intervene to ensure Cash App completes its investigation and issues the full refund owed to me. I am prepared to provide copies of all correspondence with Cash App to demonstrate the repeated delays and lack of resolution. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX

I was giving XXXX from my sister in law and it ended up in another person account I tried to contacting cash app and they denied me a few times and I also tried to send proof of what they asked and still denied me. They hung up in my face a lot of times and they say they are helping me but giving me the same run around to come to find out they are not helping me with this matter. The cash I was scammed by is ( XXXX XXXX

I received a call at XXXX on XX/XX/year>. The caller ID showed it was my daughter who lives in XXXX. I answered, heard who I thought was my daughter cry mom then shaky breathing. A man with a deep voice took the phone from her. He told me not to call anyone or the police. All I had to do was pay him and he wouldn't hurt her. I could hear crying in the background. I told him I didn't have any money. He asked, you don't have any money? I said no, I'm a XXXX  and don't have any money. He said you have {$500.00}? I said I could find it. He said I needed to send it via Cash App or another app that I didn't understand what he said. He started yelling and told me to hurry up because he didn't want to hurt her but he would. He said once I paid and he saw the money in his account he would set the phone in her lap and leave. I said I was looking for a pen to write the cash app down. He yelled again and told me he was going to beat her, that I didn't need to write it down, just enter it. I asked to speak to her. He yelled and threatened to beat her again. He then gave me his Cash App ( XXXX - XXXX XXXX was the name that came up ). I sent the {$500.00}. He said he was going to set the phone down in her lap and leave now. The line was on for about 5 more minutes but no one was talking and I didn't hear anything. I kept calling my daughter 's name then the phone call disconnected. I was on text with XXXX. When the call disconnected they told me to call XXXX XXXX to request a welfare check. I called and they out me through to XXXX XXXX. I gave my report and asked for a call back when the police did the check. They called about 2 hours later. They didn't hear anything inside the apartment so were not able to go inside. After another hour, my daughter finally called me. She was fine and the call didn't come from her. After talking to her I looked up and realized there are scams where the contact number was spoofed.

This is a serious case of Regulation E violations and UDAAP ( Unfair, Deceptive, or Abusive Acts or Practices ). Hanging up on a customer mid-report and misfiling disputes to trigger automatic denials are direct violations of these protections.

case_records.product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
case_records.narrative: I was scammed in XXXX. I sent a total of {$300.00} to a guy via Cash App who turned out to be a total fraud. I filed claims on the XXXX transactions I had with that account and Cash App agreed - yes, I was scammed, and reimbursed most of my money. I am attaching screenshots of those XXXX claims below that clearly show that Cash App AGREED/CONFIRMED that I was scammed and REIMBURSED {$250.00} of the {$300.00}. Now why do I keep getting rejected on this last {$50.00} claim? It makes no sense. And when I BEG Disputes for information or reasons, I've only heard XXXX responses : XXXX. " You authorized the payment so your claim has been denied. '' This isn't a valid argument. Of course I authorized the payment, I authorized all XXXX of them because I didn't know I was being scammed at the time! But the other XXXX transactions were reimbursed so clearly that argument can't be used since Cash App already showed me that they can reimburse authorized transactions if they prove to be a scam. Another version of this is that " no error occurred '', but again, this isn't about authorization or an error, this is about a SCAM which Cash App ALREADY AGREED with as shown in the pics I'm attaching.

case_records.product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
case_records.issue: Managing, opening, or closing your mobile wallet account
case_records.narrative:
My Cash App account and Cash App Card were recently deactivated. I received an email stating that my Cash App Card was deactivated and that any future paychecks sent to my account would be returned.

I have had this account for approximately three years and have only used it for normal personal transactions such as sending money to friends or helping with personal expenses.

When I contacted Cash App support through both chat and phone, I was told that my account violated their Terms of Service. However, no representative was able or willing to explain what specific action or transaction violated their policies. I was simply told that the decision was final.

The only transaction that may have been misunderstood was a payment I sent to a friend to help them pay a pawn shop loan payment. In the payment note I wrote {$100.00} for food and {$140.00} XXXX loan payment. This wording referred to helping a friend make a loan payment on an item held at a pawn shop. I was not purchasing, selling, or transferring a firearm or any restricted item. 

It is possible that this wording was misunderstood by an automated system reviewing payment notes. If so, I respectfully request that Cash App re

I also submitted an XXXX XXXX Report # [ REDACTED ]. The experience has been far has been far ideal : Failure to Investigate & Inconsistent Outcomes : Despite all transactions stemming from the same incident and evidence, Cash App randomly closed portions of the investigation without notifying me or providing a substantive explanation, while others remain open. This demonstrates that a " thorough and timely investigation '' was not performed.Failure to Provide Provisional Credit : It has been over XXXX business days since my report and a provisional credit for the {$1600.00} has not been issued.Inadequate XXXX XXXX  : My attempts to use your " XXXX live-person support '' resulted in agents closing chats prematurely without re-opening cases or ensuring the attached FBI XXXX documentation was reviewed.

assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 30 consumers affected by the same pattern at Block, Inc., and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: algorithm, automated decision, automated system, chatbot.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: actions taken without notice, automated loops, decisions given with no explanation, no human reachable.
The pattern repeats across 30 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Accounts/cards are actioned without notice, denying customers the chance to respond before harm crystallises.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: Automated AML / sanctions screening is likely freezing withdrawals; a recent model change may have increased false positives on legitimate users.
hypotheses.evidence: 30 complaints showing withdrawal-freeze and verification-failure cues; concentrated at Block, Inc..

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to Block, Inc. for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask Block, Inc. directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated monitoring model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

actions.action: Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

clusters.name: Credit Reporting — Inquiries, Fraudulent, Identity — Incorrect information on your report
sample_narratives:
I am submitting this Identity Theft Report because credit inquiries and accounts were opened in my name without my knowledge, authorization, or consent. I am the victim of identity theft, and this statement explains how I discovered the fraud, what actions I have takenincluding filing a police reportand why the fraudulent accounts and inquiries must be removed from my credit file. Everything provided in this statement is accurate to the best of my knowledge. 

I first realized something was wrong when I reviewed my credit report and noticed several inquiries and account-related activities that were completely unfamiliar. These inquiries came from lenders and companies with which I have never applied, interacted, or shared my personal information. Before seeing these entries, I had no reason to believe my identity had been compromised. I have always taken care to protect my personal information, so seeing unauthorized inquiries immediately raised concern. After confirming that I had not applied for any of the accounts listed, it became evident that I was a victim of identity theft. 

None of the accounts or credit applications reflected on my report were initiated or approved by me. At no point have I given permission for anyone else to use my personal details, including my name, Social Security number, birthdate, address, or financial information. These inquiries appeared suddenly and without any legitimate connection to my activities. I have been actively monitoring my credit for accuracy, and the presence of these entries clearly indicated fraudulent use of my information. 

Once I discovered the unauthorized activity, I took immediate action. I contacted the lenders associated with the inquiries to inform them that the applications were fraudulent and that I did not submit or authorize any of them. I provided proof of my identity when required and requested that they close, cancel, or flag these applications as identity theft. These creditors were notified that I
