case_records.product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
case_records.narrative:
A few weeks ago I reviewed my credit report and discovered several accounts and hard inquiries that I never authorized. There is no document proving that I ever gave permission for these entities to access my information or report anything on my file.

When I saw this, I became concerned because it had already affected me without my knowledge. I was denied a loan I needed, and on another financing I received a higher interest rate than I should have. I was being harmed by this situation.

I did what the law requires I filed a report with the FTC and submitted it directly to the credit bureau. Under FCRA 605B, when a consumer submits that report, the bureau has four business days to block the disputed information, unless it can prove with actual documentation that the consumer authorized those accounts. In my case, that proof does not exist, because I never authorized anything.

Despite completing the entire process, the information is still appearing on my report. The bureau has not shown me any evidence that I authorized those accounts or inquiries.

Furthermore, FCRA 604 is clear : no one can access my credit history without a permissible purpose. These inquiries were made withou

I am the victim of identity theft, and this statement explains how I discovered the fraud, what actions I have takenincluding filing a police reportand why the fraudulent accounts and inquiries must be removed from my credit file. Everything provided in this statement is accurate to the best of my knowledge. 

I first realized something was wrong when I reviewed my credit report and noticed several inquiries and account-related activities that were completely unfamiliar. These inquiries came from lenders and companies with which I have never applied, interacted, or shared my personal information. Before seeing these entries, I had no reason to believe my identity had been compromised. I have always taken care to protect my personal information, so seeing unauthorized inquiries immediately raised concern. After confirming that I had not applied for any of the accounts listed, it became evident that I was a victim of identity theft. 

None of the accounts or credit applications reflected on my report were initiated or approved by me.

case_records.product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
case_records.narrative:
A few weeks ago I reviewed my credit report and discovered several accounts and hard inquiries that I never authorized. There is no document proving that I ever gave permission for these entities to access my information or report anything on my file. 
When I saw this, I became concerned because it had already affected me without my knowledge. I was denied a loan I needed, and on another financing I received a higher interest rate than I should have. I was being harmed by this situation.

I did what the law requires I filed a report with the FTC and submitted it directly to the credit bureau. Under FCRA 605B, when a consumer submits that report, the bureau has four business days to block the disputed information, unless it can prove with actual documentation that the consumer authorized those accounts. In my case, that proof does not exist, because I never authorized anything.

Despite completing the entire process, the information is still appearing on my report. The bureau has not shown me any evidence that I authorized those accounts or inquiries.

Furthermore, FCRA 604 is clear : no one can access my credit history without a permissible purpose. These inquiries were made withou

case_records.product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
case_records.narrative:
A few weeks ago I reviewed my credit report and discovered several accounts and hard inquiries that I never authorized. There is no document proving that I ever gave permission for these entities to access my information or report anything on my file. 
When I saw this, I became concerned because it had already affected me without my knowledge. I was denied a loan I needed, and on another financing I received a higher interest rate than I should have. I was being harmed by this situation. 
I did what the law requires I filed a report with the FTC and submitted it directly to the credit bureau. Under FCRA 605B, when a consumer submits that report, the bureau has four business days to block the disputed information, unless it can prove with actual documentation that the consumer authorized those accounts. In my case, that proof does not exist, because I never authorized anything. 
Despite completing the entire process, the information is still appearing on my report. The bureau has not shown me any evidence that I authorized those accounts or inquiries. 
Furthermore, FCRA 604 is clear : no one can access my credit history without a permissible purpose. These inquiries were made withou

assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 54 consumers affected by the same pattern at TRANSUNION INTERMEDIATE HOLDINGS, INC. and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: decisions given with no explanation, no human reachable.
The pattern repeats across 54 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.
Customers cannot reach a human to question or reverse an automated decision — an unreasonable barrier to support and redress.

hypotheses.mechanism: Automated dispute resolution (e-OSCAR-style) is likely closing investigations without genuine human review, leaving errors uncorrected.
hypotheses.evidence: 54 complaints showing automated-investigation and unresolved-dispute cues; concentrated at TRANSUNION INTERMEDIATE HOLDINGS, INC., EQUIFAX, INC., Experian Information Solutions Inc..

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to TRANSUNION INTERMEDIATE HOLDINGS, INC. for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask TRANSUNION INTERMEDIATE HOLDINGS, INC. directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated decisioning model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

actions.action: Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 38 consumers affected by the same pattern at Paypal Holdings, Inc, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: decisions given with no explanation.
The pattern repeats across 38 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: Automated AML / sanctions screening is likely freezing withdrawals; a recent model change may have increased false positives on legitimate users.
hypotheses.evidence: 38 complaints showing withdrawal-freeze and verification-failure cues; concentrated at Paypal Holdings, Inc.

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to Paypal Holdings, Inc for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask Paypal Holdings, Inc directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated monitoring model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

clusters.name: Mortgages — Make, Documents, Representative — Trouble during payment process
sample_narratives: I am a lendee with HomeloanServ on a repayment plan with the lender. I attempted numerous times to make payment before deadline and was met with great barriers with their payment options just to make a payment and believe they are intentionally doing this in bad faith to roll lendees into ongoing repayment plans. I have voiced my concern with representatives prior about the issue with their payment options which have been ignored. The only option to repay is via phone with no online options. On XX/XX/XXXX I attempted to make payment with their automated system via phone and was met with an error. On XX/XX/XXXX & XX/XX/XXXX I tried to contact a representative with no answer as well as attempt to use their automated system. All 3 days did not work, when I was finally able to contact a HomeLoanServ representative on XXXX ( after waiting on hold for over 1 hour ), I was told that since I did not complete payment before deadline that my repayment plan has been rejected and that I would need to apply for a new repayment plan. When I explained that I tried to make payment numerous times before the deadline and this should be investigated or appealed, I was advised that I can only speak to a supervisor. After being on hold again, I was then told by the HomeLoanServ representative that there too many escalations from others to speak to their supervisors and I would have to wait possibly 3 days to receive a response from a supervisor. I informed the representative to note that I already attempted to pay dues prior to deadline and HomeloanServ failed to successfully give me options for paying plan successfully with errors in automated sytem as well as unable to speak with reps. I am filing a complaint with HomeloanServ due to their bad faith business practice and intentionally ignoring payment complications with their systems and then penalizing the lendees. Not sure if this is the first complaint, but I am voicing this concern with them.

I was laid off from both my jobs in XXXX and was unemployed since XX/XX/XXXX, which resulted in our mortgage going into a forbearance plan. I made a significant amount of our monthly income before being laid off. There have been major shifts in my industry, which I hold a XXXX XXXX and fifteen years experience in. I was not able to get a job in my area of expertise and have just started working a retail job. 

However, during my forbearance plan with my mortgage company, XXXX XXXX XXXX, my case has been mishandled. I have called every time our forbearance was up to apply for extensions and kept in constant contact with XXXX to show in good faith we were trying everything to keep our house. We have been assigned three different case managers in this time, and never been able to speak to one of them. Every time we spoke to a representative, we were given different information such as our forbearance plan could last up to 18 months, and right at the 12 month mark, found out this was untrue and we had to begin paying immediately. 

As our forbearance would be up XX/XX/XXXX, I continued to make contact with XXXX during XXXX to request a loan modification. On XX/XX/XXXX at XXXXXXXX XXXX MST, I spoke to two agents who both just verified my information and then told me they could do nothing to help and would submit a call back. I never received that call back. I went out of my way again to call and speak to someone since I never received a callback. I spoke to an agent on XX/XX/XXXX who was extremely unhelpful and did not want to submit any paperwork for us to get started. I sent an email ( see attached screenshot ) with documents they needed on XX/XX/XXXX at XXXX MST, and requested another call back from a manager. Once again, never received that call back, and received paperwork we needed to fill out during XXXX, which we can not submit during a holiday. 

Today, yet again I have called XXXX  to speak to a manager. I had to call three times ( see screenshots ) just to spe

Beginning around 3032 days past due, I attempted multiple times to make my XXXX payment, but every payment channel blocked me. In addition, during the last week of XXXX, I again attempted to make my XXXX payment before the 60-day mark. I was sick with a XXXX XXXX  and was not able to even pick up the phone throughout XXXX. Despite being ill, I still attempted to pay through the automated phone system, the online portal, and the guest pay function and all of them continued to fail before XX/XX/XXXX. 
This shows that I made repeated, good-faith efforts to cure the delinquency before 60 days, and that it was the servicers system failuresnot my actionsthat prevented payment. 
After failed attempts I emailed XXXX customer care team around XX/XX/XXXX XXXX am explain the issues. I did not receive a response until XX/XX/XXXX. There were contact information that was inaccurate and other reps from XXXX expressed I was told misleading information.

For the past several weeks, I have called XXXX XXXX numerous times in an attempt to submit payment. Harm Caused XXXX XXXX  failure to provide a functional payment method has caused and continues to cause the following harm : I am being prevented from making a good-faith mortgage payment Late fees and penalties are likely accruing due solely to Selenes inaction My credit may be negatively impacted despite my documented attempts to pay I am exposed to unnec

On XX/XX/year>, I spoke with a manager named XXXX who alleged that Payment Deferral option is not available for my husbands VA loan. When I asked her to please provide any documents that show this policy that VA loans are not able to do a Payment Deferral, she refused to do so and hung up on me. 
I called back and spoke with XXXX who said an algorithm decides whether we qualify for Payment Deferral option, and refused to provide me with the information as to what documents are required and criteria considered for this option.

I escalated this issue to a second manager by the alleged name of XXXX XXXX, who stated that Payment Deferral option is available for VA loans but only for XXXX XXXX XXXX members. When I told him that my husband was XXXX and XXXX XXXX in XXXX, he then switched back to saying that Payment Deferral is not available for all VA loans. They all refused to provide me with documents that show that VA loans do not qualify for Payment Deferral option. 
I have reason to believe that we are being targeted because of my husbands military status.

I have called them 4 times waited on hold for about 30 minutes each time to get hung up on every time. Loan payments to Mr. Cooper Mortgage. I was charged twice on XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX despite setting up automatic payments. My attempts to contact the company were initially unsuccessful due to their automated system, which did not allow me to speak to a representative. Eventually, I managed to speak to a representative who suggested a three-way call with my bank to resolve the issue and return my money. However, during the call, the representative disconnected, forcing me to repeat the process. After more attempts, the website went down, adding to my frustration.

I have been unable to make my XXXX payment and today is XXXX XXXX XXXX in a few days my account will be late for the first time EVER. 
When I call the XXXX XXXX phone number their automated system prevents me from accessing any information or speaking speaking with any real person. The automated system keeps running me around and NEVER gives me the option of getting through to a customer service representative and instead sends me to someplace called Rocket Mortgage that has an automated system that gives me the same run around with NEVER being able to speak to a representative. 
If this isn't already illegal it should be.

I recently refinanced my loan from XXXX XXXX XXXX  XXXX ) to another company. Shortly after my Refinance, XXXX merged their site with rocketmortgage. It has taken weeks to get my tax documents. XXXX sent an email saying they were available online but would not be mailed. Fine. I attempted to login and get ( depending on the day ) : Wrong password, something has gone wrong, or account not found. Fine. Account issues happen. The problem I have is that it is impossible to get ahold of anyone to work on these issues. Calling their customer service line leads to a prompt saying " your loan has been paid in full please say one of these options : payoff notice, tax documents, etc. I have tried every option and they all lead to the automated system telling me to login to get my documents. There is no way to get someone on the phone if your loan has been paid off. All Roads lead to their website which doesn't work. No way to talk to someone about login issues on their website. Trying to use the customer support chat on their website leads to a " talk to a live representative button '' that when clicked says we are busy. login to chat with a representative or try again another time. I even l

I have been attempting to resolve an issue with my mortgage for several weeks and have been unable to get Truist to communicate with me despite repeated efforts. Following the death of the borrower, my husband, ownership of the property was legally transferred through probate over seven years ago. Despite this, Truist has required Successor in Interest documentation and has refused to speak with me about the loan until this is processed. I submitted the requested documentation, but Truist continued to delay processing and requested additional items in a piecemeal manner rather than all at once. Most recently, I was told to resubmit part of a form and then later told I needed to prove that XXXX XXXX and XXXX XXXX XXXX are the same person, which could have been requested earlier but was not. During this time, I repeatedly attempted to contact Truist by phone and email to obtain basic information, including the reinstatement amount and confirmation of my loan status. My emails have gone unanswered, and when I call, I am told they can not speak with me due to the documentation issue. The delinquency was caused by a minor autopay discrepancy in which my monthly payment increased from {$

XX/XX/2026 I am not able to log on to my account to access my tax documents. I successfully reset my password, but the system still will not let me in. Chat is not available, the toll-free numbers give me XXXX un-useful options ( all direct me to log on to my online account ), then hangs up on me. It is a Sunday, so I don't expect a live person. 

XX/XX/2026 I call XXXX different toll free numbers and try to use the chat feature on their website multiple times. I am able to talk to XXXX different customer service representatives that transfer me to the save voice automated system that gives me XXXX un-useful options, then hangs up on me. The XXXX options all involve logging in to the website. 

The system tells me I have been locked out. It tells me to reset my password or contact Customer Support. The 'reset my password ' function does not give me access. There is NO email or phone number provided with the contact customer support suggestion. Going to the " Contact Us '' page only gives me a physical address. 

I found various phone numbers using a general web search engine. These are the ones I called : XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, XXXX.

assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 18 consumers affected by the same pattern at Rocket Mortgage, LLC and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: algorithm, automated loop, automated system, chatbot.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: automated loops, decisions given with no explanation, no human reachable.
The pattern repeats across 18 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Customers are trapped in automated loops with no escalation path, frustrating their ability to resolve the issue.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: Automated underwriting / affordability logic or an automated rate-switching process appears to be producing detriment without a clear, individualised rationale.
hypotheses.evidence: 18 complaints showing automated underwriting and rate-switch cues; concentrated at Rocket Mortgage, LLC, Selene Holdings LLC, Shellpoint Partners, LLC.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to Rocket Mortgage, LLC for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask Rocket Mortgage, LLC directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated decisioning model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

clusters.name: Credit Reporting — Life, Just, Hard — Problem with a company's investigation into an existing problem
sample_narratives:
This situation has completely turned my life upside down. I discovered someone used my information to open accounts and damage my credit, and its been an emotional and financial nightmare. Every time I try to explain to the credit bureaus or the companies involved, Im met with silence or excuses. Im not just a number Im a real person trying to recover from something I didnt cause. Please help me get these fraudulent items removed so I can rebuild my life and protect my future.

I'm writing because my identity has been stolen, and it has caused me more stress than I could have ever imagined. Ive worked hard to build my credit and protect my name, but now Im being held responsible for accounts and debts that arent mine. Ive done everything I can to prove my innocence, but I feel like Im being ignored by the companies reporting this false information. I just want my name cleared and my peace of mind back. No one should have to fight this hard to be seen as themselves again.

assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 51 consumers affected by the same pattern at TRANSUNION INTERMEDIATE HOLDINGS, INC. and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: no human reachable.
The pattern repeats across 51 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Customers cannot reach a human to question or reverse an automated decision — an unreasonable barrier to support and redress.

hypotheses.mechanism: Automated dispute resolution (e-OSCAR-style) is likely closing investigations without genuine human review, leaving errors uncorrected.
hypotheses.evidence: 51 complaints showing automated-investigation and unresolved-dispute cues; concentrated at TRANSUNION INTERMEDIATE HOLDINGS, INC., EQUIFAX, INC., Experian Information Solutions Inc..

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to TRANSUNION INTERMEDIATE HOLDINGS, INC. for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask TRANSUNION INTERMEDIATE HOLDINGS, INC. directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated decisioning model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

actions.action: Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

clusters.name: Banking — Chase, Check, Closed — Closing an account
sample_narratives:
Subject : Chase Bank Wrongfully Withholding My Funds and Mishandling Fraud Request for Return of Funds and XXXX XXXX : JPMorgan Chase Issue : Fraud / Account Closure / Failure to Return Consumer Funds Amount Withheld : {$900.00} ( my personal funds ) Account Status : Closed by Chase on or around XX/XX/XXXX My Name : XXXX XXXX Account number : XXXX I am filing this complaint because JPMorgan Chase is wrongfully withholding {$900.00} of my personal funds following a fraud incident involving my lost debit card. Chase has mishandled the situation, failed to process a proper fraud claim, closed my account without documentation, and continues to withhold my money without justification. 

I lost my debit card, which unfortunately had the PIN written on the back. An unauthorized individual used my card information to attempt fraud by trying to deposit a {$17000.00} check with my personal details written on it. 
I want to be clear : I did not deposit this check I did not authorize any such transaction I never touched or withdrew any funds related to this attempted fraud I contacted Chase multiple times and requested to file a fraud claim, but I was transferred repeatedly and told no one could assist me. Chase closed my account and only sent a final balance statement no account-closure letter, no fraud documentation, nothing explaining the decision. 

I was originally told that my remaining balance of {$900.00} would be mailed to me within XXXX business days. 
However, when I called for an update on XX/XX/year>, I was told : My funds would not be released The delay was due to an open investigation involving the fraudulent check I would need to wait indefinitely until the check issuers bank finished its review Chase could not provide a timeline or any written explanation The fraudulent {$17000.00} check has nothing to do with my personal funds. There is no legal basis for Chase to hold my {$900.00}. I have been left without access to my own money, with no updates, documentatio

On XX/XX/XXXX, I deposited {$1900.00} cash at a Chase ATM. The ATM printed only a small partial slip, so I immediately contacted Chase. They provided a temporary credit of {$1900.00}, and I believed the issue was resolved. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, Chase reversed the temporary credit and withdrew the full {$1900.00} from my account. 

I contacted Chase again on XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX to ask why the claim was denied, since I had the deposit slip. I was told to submit the slip, which I did.

Complaint to the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ) I am filing a formal complaint against JPMorgan Chase Bank regarding their handling of a dispute I submitted approximately XXXX  days ago. Chase claims that their investigation has been completed ; however, I was never provided with any written notice of the outcome, nor was I given a fair or reasonable opportunity to respond or submit supporting evidence. 

Throughout this process, Chase failed to provide an equitable method for submitting documentation. I was instructed to fax materials, yet I never received any confirmation or acknowledgment that my evidence was received or reviewed. Additionally, Chase was aware that I did not have access to my previous account and could not upload documents electronically, as I had communicated this multiple times. Despite this, they made no reasonable accommodations to ensure I could participate in the investigation process. 

I am formally demanding that Chase reopen this investigation and provide me with a fair opportunity to submit evidence, respond to any claims, and fully participate in the dispute process as required by law. 

The underlying dispute involves an airline XXXX

case_records.narrative:
I am filing this complaint regarding how Chase Bank handled my ACH dispute involving a {$790.00} transaction from XXXX I initially disputed this transaction with Chase Bank on the basis that the charge was not valid as billed. While Chase issued a provisional credit, they later reversed that credit after determining the transaction was authorized. 
The issue is not authorization alone. 
The issue is that the merchant did not deliver the contracted services and misrepresented what was provided. 
XXXX claims that services began immediately and were delivered. However : No functional website, system, or usable service was implemented No completed deliverables were provided No meaningful service capable of generating patient communication or business value was operational The only action taken was insertion of a chatbot-style line of code into third-party platforms ( XXXX / XXXX ) XXXX which does not constitute delivery of contracted services The merchant is using minimal or non-functional setup activity to justify full contractual obligation under a XXXX agreement Additionally, there were representations made during the sales process implying integration and performance with platforms

assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 16 consumers affected by the same pattern at JPMORGAN CHASE & CO., and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: algorithm, automated system, chatbot, glitch.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: actions taken without notice, automated loops, decisions given with no explanation, no human reachable.
The pattern repeats across 16 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Accounts/cards are actioned without notice, denying customers the chance to respond before harm crystallises.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: The firm likely tightened an automated transaction-monitoring / financial-crime model, raising false-positive exit rates — legitimate customers are 'de-risked' and offboarded without manual review.
hypotheses.evidence: 16 complaints showing account-closure and suspicious-activity language concentrated at a few firms; concentrated at JPMORGAN CHASE & CO..

hypotheses.mechanism: An automated fraud-detection model may be flagging legitimate transactions, freezing funds before any human check.
hypotheses.evidence: 16 complaints showing fund-freeze and access-loss cues; concentrated at JPMORGAN CHASE & CO..

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to JPMORGAN CHASE & CO. for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask JPMORGAN CHASE & CO. directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated monitoring model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

actions.action: Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

I used XXXX XXXX money remittance, and I found out that someone used a different telephone number under my own name and e-mail address to take {$1900.00} dollars out of my Wells Fargo account. I have submitted document to the fact that I don't know the telephone number and that I have not authorized such transactions. 
The company keeps brushing off my request and my concerns. 
I have filed a police report with XXXX Police Department and Police Report # is XXXX. 
I have also attempted Wells Fargo and I keep getting same run around.

case_records.product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
case_records.narrative:
On XX/XX/year>, a {$300.00} XXXX transaction was sent from my Wells Fargo account as the result of a scam. I did not receive any goods or services in exchange for this payment. 

I promptly contacted Wells Fargo to report the transaction as fraudulent and requested that the issue be investigated. However, Wells Fargo did not provide a confirmation number, written acknowledgment, investigation status, or final determination. I received no follow-up communication despite my attempt to resolve the issue directly with the bank. 

As a result, the dispute was effectively abandoned without explanation. Wells Fargo failed to properly investigate or communicate the outcome of my fraud report, leaving the issue unresolved. 

I reported this XXXX transaction to Wells Fargo, but no confirmation number, written acknowledgment, or documentation was provided.

I called Wells Fargo and made a fraud claim. I disactivated my cards and created a new checking/savings account. On XX/XX/XXXX, I receive a letter stating that they will be unable to honor my claim and take back the {$2400.00} in provisional credit I received. Theyre reason for denying my claims are due to the card was in your possession at the time the claim was filed and during the time of the disputed activity and There were no further card present transactions attempts after the card was closed. I dont understand this since the withdrawals were made in Florida . I would like the case to reopen and further investigation to be done because I dont understand how this doesnt look like obvious fraud. No one has access to my card or card informatio

case_records.product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
case_records.narrative: On Tuesday XX/XX/year> a fraudulent transaction ( seemingly done as a wire transfer ) was made on my savings account with Wells Fargo for {$4900.00}. It was a transaction made to a company named XXXX XXXX that was taken out of my account. Ive never heard of this company and did not authorize any transaction to be taken out of my account. I noticed the transaction when I checked my account on that Friday, XX/XX/year> and saw that my saving account was missing a large amount of money from it. I immediately tried to call Wells Fargos online phone number to report the fraudulent activity but was put on hold for almost 2 hours. I tried to call back again but got hung up on after waiting another hour. I went into my local Wells Fargo branch on Saturday, XX/XX/XXXX and spoke to a manager and banker and they did see that a wire transfer had been done on my account and they tried to help me file a claim over the phone but they too were unable to get anyone to help for over an hour. I was told to set up an appointment and come back on Monday, XX/XX/XXXX. On Monday the banker and I got back on the phone and were able to finally get someone on the line to help us with the claim for the {$4900.

case_records.product: Money transfer, virtual currency, or money service
case_records.narrative:
on Thursday, XX/XX/XXXX, at XXXXXXXX XXXX CST, I was contacted by an individual impersonating Wells Fargo Fraud Protection. The caller claimed there was suspicious activity on my account and used social engineering tactics to convince me that my funds were at risk and needed to be secured immediately.

The caller was amazingly thorough and referenced legitimate recent transactions from my checking account, which caused me to reasonably believe I was speaking with Wells Fargo. They said it was likely my card had been skimmed at a gas station and someone what using that to access my checking account. Under this false representation and deception, I was instructed to transfer {$10000.00} from my bank account to my XXXX XXXX XXXX account. 

I called wells fargo and started a claim 2 minutes after I got off the phone with the scammer when I realized i could not log into my WF account. I then called XXXX XXXX to report the fraud - all while the transaction was still " pending '' hoping desperately they could trace the money and claw it back.

once they had control of my accounts they sent the money to a XXXX merchant account the transaction was described as " XXXX '', the XXXX XXXX repre

assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 13 consumers affected by the same pattern at WELLS FARGO & COMPANY, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: automated loops, decisions given with no explanation, no human reachable.
The pattern repeats across 13 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Customers are trapped in automated loops with no escalation path, frustrating their ability to resolve the issue.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: Automated AML / sanctions screening is likely freezing withdrawals; a recent model change may have increased false positives on legitimate users.
hypotheses.evidence: 13 complaints showing withdrawal-freeze and verification-failure cues; concentrated at WELLS FARGO & COMPANY.

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to WELLS FARGO & COMPANY for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask WELLS FARGO & COMPANY directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated monitoring model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

actions.action: Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

clusters.name: Mortgages — Rocket, Mortgage, Rocket Mortgage — Trouble during payment process
sample_narratives:
In XX/XX/XXXX, I was notified that my mortgage servicer had changed from XXXX XXXX to Rocket Mortgage. Shortly thereafter, I received correspondence referencing XXXX XXXX, which caused confusion and concern because I had been told Rocket Mortgage had acquired my mortgage, not XXXX XXXX. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I called Rocket Mortgage to inquire about who XXXX XXXX was and because I had also received a collections call from Rocket Mortgage attempting to collect a debt during the servicing transfer. During that call, I explicitly informed Rocket Mortgage that, due to concerns about fraud and the servicing transition, I did not want to be placed on paperless billing and wanted to remain on paper billing. This was discussed directly with a Rocket Mortgage representative. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I called Rocket Mortgage at XXXX to make a payment. I attempted to make a payment for XXXX and then make a separate payment for XXXX. However, the automated system would not allow a partial or separate payment and required me to make both payments together. I ultimately made the combined payment because the system would not allow any other option. 

Immediately after the payment was processed, the automated system informed me that I had been placed on paperless billing, despite my prior explicit request not to be enrolled in paperless billing. 

I stayed on the line and spoke with a Rocket Mortgage representative. I was told that the only way to be removed from paperless billing and return to paper statements was to create an online account. I informed the representative that I do not want to create an online account and want paper billing for my records. I was told there was no other way. 

I requested escalation and spoke with a manager, who repeated the same statement : that I must create an online account in order to receive paper billing. I again explained that during the transition I had specifically instructed Rocket Mortgage that I wanted to remain on paper billing, and that I did not

am experiencing serious issues with my mortgage servicer, ServiceMac, related to an escrow analysis and payment increase. 

For approximately two months, I have repeatedly attempted to contact ServiceMac by telephone during their stated business hours ( XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. ET ). Each time, the automated system instructs me to call during business hours, even though I am calling within that timeframe. I have been unable to reach a live representative. 

During this period, my monthly mortgage payment increased by approximately {$1000.00}, allegedly due to an escrow shortage. I have not received a clear explanation or written breakdown supporting this increase. 

I am also unable to access my account online. Despite multiple attempts to log in and resolve the issue by phone, I have been unable to gain access or receive assistance. 

On XX/XX/year>, I formally requested a written escrow accounting, including escrow disbursements for the past 24 months, in order to review the accuracy of the escrow calculation. As of today, I have not received a response. 

I am requesting that ServiceMac provide a full written escrow accounting, investigate whether the escrow analysis was calculated correctly, and correct any errors. I am also requesting assistance in restoring account access and ensuring that no adverse action is taken while this matter is under review.

I've called Wells Fargo XXXX different times now to change the auto pay on my mortgage that I've had with them since 2021. I've received multiple confirmation numbers from their phone representatives stating they have set up the auto pay. However, I keep getting emails from Wells Fargo stating there's issues with my payment and the payments aren't coming out of my bank. 

The reason I first changed the bank account was because I was closing the bank account I had used for years to pay the mortgage. I was told it was going to be processed in time. 

This did not happen. When I called back, they said the payment was reversed because it could not locate the account ( because I closed it ). 

I then set up the auto pay again and had to also pay XXXX 's payment on the phone first. I completed this and received a confirmation. 

The money was never taken out and then I needed to call again and was told there was now an issue with the one-time payment. 

All of the payments are from Wells Fargo accounts, there's always been more than enough money in the accounts to cover the payments, and this has taken time away from work having to continually call in and is creating stress budgeting wise and I do not want their incompetence to affect my credit score, payment history, etc.

My payments should come out the first of every month.

I can not keep taking the time to call in just for nothing to get taken care of. Each time you call in, you have to go through the automated system which takes forever and then talk to workers who clearly can't handle simply setting up payments. Very unprofessional and unhelpful! This needs to be addresses ASAP or I will be closing all Wells Fargo accounts / credit cards / everything.

We have assumed a mortgage for my husband 's deceased grandparents we turned all of our paperwork over to XXXX XXXX  we had full access to our account. When rocket mortgage took over the loan we have had access online no issues. They keep sending emails to set up the online account when we try to register it says this information is not found. When I call in I'm told.they don't all successors to access the account at all. We was advised we can only set up auto pay or call the automated system but not allowed any access or information on the account. I asked again why was we not informed of this and if she could send out written proof she just kept saying this call is going no where. We should of been notified that we no longer can access the mortgage information if needed, statements or year end tax info that if we have problems the only thing we can do is make payments or refinance the mortgage. I feel we should of been properly notified of this change so.that we had time to prepare for not being able to access information on a mortgage we pay each month without fail

clusters.name: Consumer Credit — Citibank, Pay, Payments — Can't stop withdrawals from your bank account
sample_narratives: I had taken out a loan for dental care. This loan consisted of several years of payments. When I made my last payment, I called the company to ensure i did not owe anything else. This was in XXXX. I spoke to a lady who informed me that my loan was paid in full and I did not owe anything else. XXXX comes and I receive a text saying my payment is past due. I call the company, they inform me that I missed a payment the year prior and it was a late fee, mind you, when I pay via the automated system it always says payment of $ XXXX including a late fee of {$0.00}. Not once did I miss a payment. I was never informed of a missed payment. After several hours on the phone, a supervisor is looking through my account and specifically says, the payment in question went through so he was unsure what the charge was for. He asked me to call back in a couple days. I have since been unable to reach them. They refuse to send me documents claiming i owe the {$50.00}. They reported it on my credit and has since caused my credit to plummet over XXXX points. At this point, if they can not make it right, we may have to go to court over the {$50.00}

On Monday, XX/XX/year>, I connected with chat on the Dave app & asked that my ACH be stopped & that they help me make arrangements to pay back my balance. The AI chatbot connected me to a human customer service rep. I explained to them that Im disabled and that I have XXXX XXXX I have a BIG copay for meds that Im going to have to make and I need my ACH stopped & to make arrangements to pay back my balance. I was told they couldnt do that because I had a balance. I told them that this is illegal, that its law that I can ask to have it stopped & they had to work with me to pay back my balance. They kept refusing and repeating that they couldnt stop the ACH because I have a balance. While I was on the phone with them, I texted my cousin, who is an attorney. He told me that what they were doing is illegal and to tell them that my attorney would be in touch and that I was going to file a complaint with you all & my state attorney generals office. I told them this. They told me that they would get me in touch with a supervisor and that the supervisor would email me. About XXXX hours later, I got an email from a supervisor repeating THE SAME INFORMATION the chat reps had. It said to reply to the email if I had any more questions. I did, but never received a reply.

PayPal stated that I could pay in 4 payments for two transactions i and I was charged in full for the items and not in 4 payments. I am pre-approved for these payments. When the company was contacted they said it must have been a cliche and they couldn't make a corrections to the computer error.

Sent a {$200.00} " customer appreciation '' check to buy silence. In the meantime, my XXXX score went from the mid XXXX down as far as XXXX range, then slowly improved into the XXXX - but damage

case_records.narrative: I have tried calling and chatting with them online to gain access to view my personal loan through Citibank and they just kept transferring me over and over. Not a single person was able to help and no one seemed to know what they are doing. I have every right to view my loan online and they won't give me access. I have never witnessed such atrocious customer service before in my life. It is still unresolved. They purposely make it impossible to get an answer and have you transferred 12 times to break you down and let it go. These practices are malicious.

assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 12 consumers affected by the same pattern at CITIBANK, N.A. and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: automated system, chatbot, computer error.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: automated loops, decisions given with no explanation, no human reachable.
The pattern repeats across 12 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Customers are trapped in automated loops with no escalation path, frustrating their ability to resolve the issue.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: An automated affordability / credit-decisioning model was likely recalibrated, raising decline rates. Because such models lean on proxy variables, there is a real risk of indirect discrimination.
hypotheses.evidence: 12 complaints showing automated decline and affordability cues; concentrated at CITIBANK, N.A., Westlake Services, LLC, Dave Operating, LLC.

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to CITIBANK, N.A. for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask CITIBANK, N.A. directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated decisioning model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

actions.action: Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

clusters.name: Consumer Credit — Charges, Documents, Year — Getting the loan
sample_narratives:
I am filing this complaint regarding deceptive and misleading auto-lending practices involving XXXX of XXXX XXXX Georgia ( an AutoNation dealership ) and AutoNation Finance. Their actions resulted in an undisclosed interest-rate increase, a missing APR on my contract, inconsistent and contradictory loan documents, and ongoing refusal to provide basic Truth in Lending Act ( TILA ) information. These practices appear predatory and suggest other consumers may also be affected.

Before the sale, the dealership quoted and confirmed in writing a 6.24 % interest rate with an expected monthly payment of {$740.00}. When I arrived on XX/XX/year> to finalize the purchase, the Finance Manager presented a higher monthly payment of {$780.00} without informing me of any rate change. During the signing process, he asked me to leave my seat and sit behind his desk, claiming that not all information was transferring for me to sign from my side. I complied but later learned this individual no longer works there. The behavior and explanation were unusual and now raise concerns about how the electronic documents were handled. 

The DocuSign contract I received contained no APR at all, which violates TILA and Regulation Zs requirement that the APR be clearly disclosed before signing. After the sale, my mailed statements and online AutoNation Finance portal also failed to display my APR or full loan balance. I was repeatedly told by customer service that AutoNation Finance does not list interest rates, making it impossible to verify the accuracy of the loan or determine whether I was being charged correctly. 

For nine months, I attempted to obtain my APR. On XX/XX/year>, AutoNation Finance finally sent a letter stating my loan carries a 7.49 % APR a rate I never agreed to and that contradicts the dealerships written confirmation of 6.24 %. This unexplained rate increase is inconsistent with the payment terms presented at the time I made my decision to purchase the vehicle. I attempted to

I am filing this complaint against American Express ( Amex ) regarding a failed disbursement of a {$25000.00} personal loan ( Account ending in XXXX ) and the subsequent erroneous closure of my account. 

XXXX. Failure to Provide Factual Information During Proactive Inquiries : Leading up to the disbursement, I proactively contacted American Express numerous times to verify that my banking information was correct and to inquire why the funds had not yet arrived. During every call, representatives failed to identify any issues and repeatedly instructed me to " wait it out, '' assuring me the funds were on the way. 

XXXX. Contradictory Instructions and Erroneous Closure : On XX/XX/year>, I received an official email from American Express stating they were unable to identify a valid bank account for a " payment '' of {$25000.00} on XX/XX/year>. This email explicitly instructed me to go to americanexpress.com/pbc to update my banking information to continue with the loan. However, when I attempted to follow these instructions the very next morning, I discovered the loan account had already been cancelled and marked as " Paid in Full. '' XXXX. Systematic Reconciliation Error : It appears that when my bank returned the {$25000.00} disbursement due to an ACH error, the Amex automated system incorrectly flagged this return as a " voluntary payment '' rather than a " failed disbursement. '' This triggered an automatic satisfaction of the loan and a permanent closure of the account, effectively blocking me from the funds I contractually signed for. 

XXXX. Supervisory Negligence and Evidence Obstruction : On XX/XX/year>, I spoke with a supervisor named XXXX. He admitted the system showed the returned funds but claimed there was " nothing he could do '' to fix the error. He refused to provide a last name or a case number. Furthermore, XXXX followed my suggestion to remove the closed account from my online dashboard without advising me that this would strip away my access to t

I applied for a loan through Sofi on XX/XX/year> I was approved and the primary applicant signed the loan documents. when it came to me as the co-applicant to sign the documents for some reason, I was not able to access the documents. I troubleshoot it with several agents on several different occasions in order to try to get this resolved, but no one was able to resolve this for me, and I kept getting the runaround still to this very moment. I am unable to access the loan documents, which is preventing me from getting the funding for the loan.

case_records.narrative:
I am filing this complaint against Klover ( XXXX ) for refusing to honor my lawful revocation of ACH and debit authorization. 
On XX/XX/year>, I lost my job and had no funds available. My Klover advance repayment was scheduled for the same day ( XX/XX/year> ). At approximately XXXX XXXX, I contacted Klover Support through their web chat and explicitly requested that my ACH authorization and debit card authorization be revoked immediately because I could not make the payment until I receive borrowed funds on XX/XX/year>. I provided my full name, email, and phone number. 
Klovers automated system transferred the request to a live agent. At XXXX XXXX, agent XXXX from the Klover XXXX XXXX XXXX responded and denied my revocation request, stating : Klover requires at least 3 business days before your payment is due in order for us to process your revoke request Due to our Terms and Conditions your revoke request has been denied. 
At XXXX XXXX  the same day, I sent Klover a formal written revocation notice by email stating : Under the Electronic Fund Transfer Act ( 15 U.S. Code 1693e ) and the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ) regulations, I, as the account holder, have the le

assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 8 consumers affected by the same pattern at SOFI TECHNOLOGIES, INC. and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: automated system, chatbot.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: automated adverse actions, automated loops, decisions given with no explanation, no human reachable.
The pattern repeats across 8 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Automated adverse actions are taken at scale, raising doubt that the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes.
Customers are trapped in automated loops with no escalation path, frustrating their ability to resolve the issue.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: An automated affordability / credit-decisioning model was likely recalibrated, raising decline rates. Because such models lean on proxy variables, there is a real risk of indirect discrimination.
hypotheses.evidence: 8 complaints showing automated decline and affordability cues; concentrated at SOFI TECHNOLOGIES, INC., CIG FINANCIAL LLC, AMERICAN EXPRESS COMPANY.

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to SOFI TECHNOLOGIES, INC. for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask SOFI TECHNOLOGIES, INC. directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated decisioning model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

actions.action: Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

clusters.name: Consumer Credit — Check, Finance, Paper — Problem when making payments
sample_narratives:
Service Finance Company Payoff Issue We had an 18-month interest-free loan with Service Finance Company , LLC . We made the required regular monthly payments of {$450.00} for 17 months through our credit union 's bill pay system. All payments were sent electronically to Service Finance Company , LLC, not as checks. 

On XX/XX/year>, knowing the XXXX payment would pay off the loan or trigger a finance charge, we called Service Finance to request the payoff amount which was {$8100.00}, due XX/XX/year>. We updated the payment amount in our credit union 's bill pay system accordingly. 

On XX/XX/year>, the credit union sent the {$8100.00} payoff as a paper check instead of electronically. We were unaware of this change and had no reason to expect it after 17 prior electronic payments. Eight days seemed sufficient for mail delivery. 

On XX/XX/year> ( the due date ), the payment did not show as posted. We called Service Finance ; the representative advised waiting until Monday or Tuesday due to the XXXX holiday and assured us the company could waive any interest for such delays. No option was offered to pay immediately over the phone. We followed their advice and waited. 

On XX/XX/year>, the payment still did not show. We called again and were initially told interest could not be waived. After being placed on hold, we were informed it could be waived if we paid the full {$8100.00} over the phone immediately. We authorized an electronic payment for that amount and were told the loan would show as paid in full later that day ( which it did ). Believing the electronic payment had resolved the payoff, we placed a stop payment on the mailed check. 

On XX/XX/year>, we discovered the electronic draw was only {$5300.00}. The Service Finance portal also showed the mailed check had been recorded on XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX to what we were told on XX/XX/XXXX. We called Service Finance, explained the error, and arranged a refund of the {$5300.00} ( since the check was now in process ). We

I took a home equity loan from XXXX bank and they required I pay off two other loans -- one of which was a personal loan with Barclays Bank. The titling agency mailed me a paper check to send to Barclays which I immediately sent via USPS as soon as I received the paper check. I have the USPS tracking receipt and the paper check was delivered last month. I have contacted Barclays many times to understand where the check is and why is has not been applied to my account. Meanwhile, interest is accruing and I am making additional payments. The irony is that XXXX wanted this loan to be paid off in full before taking out this new home equity loan and now I have two loans open both accruing interest. I maintain a XXXX credit score so I take this all very seriously. No one is helping me and I keep getting the run around.

clusters.name: Debt Collection — Email, Debt, Phone — Attempts to collect debt not owed
companies: Eastern Account Systems of Connecticut, Inc.
sample_narratives:
Beginning in or around XX/XX/year>, a disputed XXXX XXXX  charge was entered into the tenant ledger through a property management software platform ( XXXX ) used by the property XXXX. Although my account simultaneously showed a credit balance and nothing for you to pay, automated notices and ledger entries continued to reflect past-due amounts. 
The disputed utility charge was treated as rent arrears and used as the basis for payment demands and eviction-related representations, despite my timely dispute and requests for clarification. I repeatedly requested a full accounting explaining how the disputed utility charge was classified, why the ledger showed both a credit and a past-due balance, and the basis for any past-due or enforcement-related notices generated by the system. 
The responses provided did not meaningfully address these discrepancies or correct the misclassification before enforcement actions proceeded. As a result, automated system-generated records were relied upon as authoritative despite notice of dispute, leading to inaccurate representations of debt allegedly owed.

I received no phone calls, letters or emails from this company. They had not notified me in any way until I found it on my credit report. I was told they had the wrong email. 
The company 's automated system disconnected on the numerous times and made it almost impossible to reach a representative. Once I was able to get through to XXXX she could only tell me who the merchant was and suggested they may be able to verify the debt and send it to me an email. I explained that it was not accurate and gave a correct time frame. I asked to speak to a supervisor numerous times and it was refused before XXXX finally disconnected the phone call on me out of frustration.

I get loads of emails from Aidvantage about a student loan debt they say they need me to pay to them. I don't even know when Aidvantage got my information. I want to know if they are legitimate, so l 've been trying to get their email in order to contact them about emailing me the information I need. 

I have looked all over the small part of their website that I can even access and there is no email info, including under the " Email '' section of their " contact us '' page. Everything you click to get to an email to contact them funnels you into a page that makes you create an account. I don't want to make an account with a company I don't know using my personal information. I just want to email them like they email me. It feels very intrusive how they have my information somehow, but I have to give them more information to maybe be allowed to reply or ask a question via email. 

I could only access an unhelpful chatbot that did not give me an email when I asked it, only another link to log in. I asked the chatbot if I could get a Debt Validation Notice, and it gave me unrelated information, gave me a link to a student loan forgiveness site that I did not ask for, and in general the chatbot did not answer any of my questions or provide any real help. I have no way to contact this company ( Aidvantage ) who claims I have to pay them money for a debt.

On XX/XX/year>, I received an email from TrueAccord Corp. attempting to collect a debt allegedly owed to XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( original creditor : XXXX XXXX ) in the amount of {$380.00}. 
The email violates the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act in the following ways : 1. MISSING VALIDATION NOTICE ( 15 U.S.C. 1692g ( a ) ) : The communication fails to include any of the required validation notice language. It does not state that the debt will be assumed valid unless disputed within 30 days. It does not state that if I dispute the debt in writing within 30 days, TrueAccord will obtain and mail verification. It does not state that I may request the original creditors name and address in writing within 30 days. Instead, the email contains only hyperlinks labeled click here and Dispute this debt without the actual required disclosures.

2. MISSING VALIDATION PERIOD END DATE ( 12 C.F.R. 1006.34 ( c ) ( 3 ) ( iii ) ) : Regulation F requires debt collectors to disclose the date they will consider the end of the validation period. This communication does not include that date.

clusters.name: Debt Collection — Reporting, Fcra, Verification — Attempts to collect debt not owed
sample_narratives:
I am filing this complaint regarding a debt being reported under the name RNT Debt with reference XXXX, which I do not recognize, do not owe, and have never authorized or entered into any agreement related to. This account has appeared on my credit report without my knowledge, consent, or lawful validation, and despite repeated efforts to obtain documentation, verification, and clarification from the involved parties, I have not received any legally required information proving the validity, ownership, or accuracy of this alleged debt.

This complaint outlines in detail what occurred, the steps I have taken, the lack of response or inadequate response from the company, and why this tradeline violates federal consumer protection laws including the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ), and other regulatory standards intended to ensure accuracy, fairness, and transparency in consumer credit reporting and debt collection.

SECTION 1 INITIAL DISCOVERY OF THE ACCOUNT I discovered the RNT Debt account while reviewing my credit report. The account appeared to show a balance and payment history that I did not recognize. I do not have any recollection of entering into an agreement with this company, nor have I ever conducted business with any creditor or service provider that matches the name " RNT Debt '' or the reference number XXXX. 

Because the account was unfamiliar and unexpected, I immediately suspected it may have been : Reported in error Assigned to the wrong consumer Created due to identity theft Incorrectly transferred Attached to someone elses data Or otherwise inaccurately associated with me The presence of this account has already had negative consequences, as inaccurate derogatory information reduces creditworthiness and affects the ability to obtain credit, housing, insurance, employment opportunities, and other essential consumer functions.

To Whom It May Concern, I am requesting clarification for the record. 

Please identify the lawful basis, evidentiary authority, and method of verification by which you assert that I stand in the position of a borrower or debtor in relation to the account you are reporting. 

Specifically : 1. What original, wet-ink contract, bearing my knowing and voluntary signature, establishes me as a borrower rather than a creditor or lender in this transaction?

2. What competent witness with first-hand knowledge verified this account, and by what exact method ( manual review versus automated system )?

3. What proof do you possess that this obligation exists within my lawful capacity, rather than as a presumption outside covenant and consent? 

For clarity, my standing is that of a lender and not a borrower. Any claim to the contrary requires strict proof. Absent such proof, continued reporting or collection constitutes an unverified and inaccurate assertion. 

Please respond with documentary evidence, not conclusions or summaries.

case_records.narrative:

XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX  XXXX Re : Method of Verification Request Account in Question : XXXX XXXX XXXX / XXXX XXXX XXXX Original Creditor : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Account Number : XXXX To Whom It May Concern : I am writing in response to the results of your recent investigation in which you stated that the above-referenced account was verified as accurate. 

Pursuant to my rights under the Fair Credit Reporting Act, 15 U.S.C. 1681i ( a ) ( 6 ) ( B ) ( iii ), I am requesting a detailed description of the method of verification used to investigate this account. 

Based on your response, no evidence was provided demonstrating that a reasonable reinvestigation was conducted. A conclusory statement of verified does not meet the requirements of the FCRA. 

Please provide the following : 1. The name, address, and telephone number of the party or parties you contacted to verify this account 2. The date ( s ) on which such verification was performed 3. The method of communication used ( e.g., e-Oscar, automated system, phone, written correspond

assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 28 consumers affected by the same pattern at Portfolio Recovery Associates, LLC and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: algorithm, automated decision, automated system.
The pattern repeats across 28 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Repeated automated detriment calls into question whether the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes (cross-cutting duty to avoid foreseeable harm).

hypotheses.mechanism: Automated dialer / letter systems may be pursuing debts not owed, with no easy route to a human to correct the record.
hypotheses.evidence: 28 complaints showing debt-not-owed and automated-contact cues; concentrated at Portfolio Recovery Associates, LLC, SYNCHRONY FINANCIAL, CL Holdings LLC.

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to Portfolio Recovery Associates, LLC for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask Portfolio Recovery Associates, LLC directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated decisioning model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

clusters.name: Credit Reporting — Entries, Corrected, Reflect — Problem with a company's investigation into an existing problem
sample_narratives:
I am writing to formally dispute the accuracy of certain late payment entries appearing on my credit report. These entries do not accurately reflect my payment history. I have previously submitted multiple requests for investigation, along with all required documentation, yet the inaccuracies have not been corrected. The continued reporting of these erroneous entries has caused me significant financial hardship and emotional XXXX. 

Under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), I am entitled to a fair, accurate, and complete credit report. This includes the right to have any information that is inaccurate, incomplete, or unverifiable promptly corrected or removed. Despite my repeated attempts to resolve this matter, these inaccurate entries continue to appear on my report without justification or adequate explanation.
