assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 23 consumers affected by the same pattern at EQUIFAX, INC. and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: decisions given with no explanation.
The pattern repeats across 23 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: Automated dispute resolution (e-OSCAR-style) is likely closing investigations without genuine human review, leaving errors uncorrected.
hypotheses.evidence: 23 complaints showing automated-investigation and unresolved-dispute cues; concentrated at EQUIFAX, INC., Experian Information Solutions Inc., TRANSUNION INTERMEDIATE HOLDINGS, INC..

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to EQUIFAX, INC. for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask EQUIFAX, INC. directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated decisioning model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.

clusters.name: Auto Lending — Payoff, Financial, Check — Problems at the end of the loan or lease
sample_narratives:
I am filing a complaint against Toyota Financial Services/Toyota Motor Credit Corporation for an unauthorized withdrawal of {$720.00} from my bank account after my auto loan was already paid off and closed. 

Toyotas own automated system states : Your account closed and paid off on XX/XX/year>. 

Despite this, Toyota Financial withdrew {$720.00} from my account on XX/XX/year>, without authorization. I never approved any additional payments after my vehicle was paid off by XXXX XXXX XXXX. 

When I contacted Toyota Financial Services, I was given contradictory and misleading information. A representative XXXX XXXXXX/XX/XXXX insisted the account was still active and refused to let me speak with a supervisor. He denied escalation multiple times, even though this involved an unauthorized withdrawal. I have been given the runaround for days and have experienced significant stress and emotional distress as a result, as well as financial hardship due to the unexpected withdrawal. 

Toyota Financial is also short {$230.00} from the original payoff check sent by XXXX XXXX XXXX, and I have not been provided any explanation for that discrepancy. 

My concerns include : Unauthorized withdrawal from a closed account Contradictory information from representatives Refusal to escalate a financial dispute Mishandling of payoff funds Emotional and financial hardship Potential unfair or deceptive practices I am requesting the CFPB require Toyota Financial Services to : Immediately refund the unauthorized {$720.00} withdrawal Correct my account to properly reflect the closed and paid-off status Address the representatives refusal to escalate Investigate Toyota Financials contradictory information and mishandling of consumer accounts In addition, I am requesting compensation for the severe distress, fear, and emotional harm this situation caused. As a single mother with a toddler, the unauthorized withdrawal and refusal to escalate left me terrified and without access to needed funds rig

I applied and was approved for refinancing of my car loan. The original loan was through the Huntington National Bank, and the refinanced loan was through XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. Huntington Bank never processed the payoff check. It was submitted via mail in an envelope provided by XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX within 7 days of payoff quote, with that date be XX/XX/year>. When I called approximately one month later to inquire why the check had not been processed, Huntington Bank was not helpful in tracking down the check. They originally requested the check number, which I did not have handy. After calling XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX and obtaining it, they then said they couldn't help without having XXXX XXXX 's account number. It's worth noting that, each time I called Huntington Bank, I had to navigate a lengthy automated system, which was completely un-user friendly. I have a full-time job, am a XXXX XXXX XXXX and am a graduate student. I do not have time to clean up Huntington 's mess, and I should not be in a position to have to do so in the first place.

Ally sent the voluntary surrender paperwork to an incorrect address, despite my correct address being on file. I was informed by Ally representatives that I could not receive corrected paperwork and had to wait until the documents expired on XX/XX/year>.

I have made repeated attempts to contact Ally Auto Finance by phone over multiple days.

My call logs show numerous outbound calls, including calls lasting over one hour and multiple calls that ended abruptly within minutes. Despite these efforts, I was unable to reach a supervisor, and calls frequently disconnected or ended through the automated system.

These records demonstrate my continued good-faith attempts to resolve this matter and Allys failure to provide meaningful access to customer service or supervision.

I was laid off from my job in XX/XX/XXXX and contacted XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX to request an extension, payment arrangement, or temporary hardship accommodation because my XXXX payment is due XX/XX/XXXX and I start my new job XX/XX/XXXX. case_records.issue: Problems at the end of the loan or lease
case_records.narrative:
I purchased my leased XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX : XXXX ) at the end of the lease term. The buyout was completed on XX/XX/XXXX, and I have the Bill of Sale as proof of full payoff. 

Nearly four years later, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ) has still not released the lien with the California XXXX. My current registration renewal notice ( due XX/XX/XXXX ) still shows the lienholder on record, confirming the lien was never released electronically through California 's ELT ( Electronic Lien and Title ) system. 

I have attempted to contact XXXX XXXX  by phone at XXXX on multiple occasions. Their automated system restricts callers to a chatbot with limited options, and after repeated attempts to reach a live agent, the system states that agents are too busy and to try again later. I have been unable to reach a human being to resolve this. 

Under California Vehicle Code Section 5753, a lienholder is required to release the lien within 21 days of payoff. XXXX XXXX  is nearly four years past this legal deadline. 

I am requesting that XXXX XXXX immediately release the lien electronically via California 's ELT system so that I can obtain a clean title for my vehicle.

I am filing this complaint due to repeated failed attempts to contact Flagship Financial Group regarding my loan and obtain basic information about my account. I began contacting the company at approximately XXXXXXXX XXXX. As of XXXXXXXX XXXX, I had placed over 12 calls in an attempt to speak with someone about my loan. Each time I reached a representative, I was immediately transferred to another department. When I was transferred to the reinstatement department, the call was disconnected. This happened approximately three times. 

When I called back, the regular customer service representatives stated they were unable to assist me and immediately transferred me again. The call would then disconnect. During one call, I was told they could not see my call in their system and therefore could not assist me. The last several calls I placed ( approximately seven ) were not answered at all. The automated system stated they were unable to accept my call. It appears my calls are being blocked, as I am no longer able to reach anyone whatsoever.

During my initial call, I asked simply for general information regarding my loan, including my overall loan details. I was told the representative

assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 8 consumers affected by the same pattern at TOYOTA MOTOR CREDIT CORPORATION and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: automated system, chatbot.
Carries the harm fingerprint of automated decisioning: automated loops, decisions given with no explanation, no human reachable.
The pattern repeats across 8 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Customers are trapped in automated loops with no escalation path, frustrating their ability to resolve the issue.
Adverse decisions are issued without an intelligible reason, so customers cannot understand or challenge them.

hypotheses.mechanism: A recently deployed or changed automated decisioning process appears to be producing the recurring detriment seen in these complaints.
hypotheses.evidence: 8 complaints showing repeating automated-handling cues; concentrated at TOYOTA MOTOR CREDIT CORPORATION, Westlake Services, LLC, HUNTINGTON NATIONAL BANK, THE.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to TOYOTA MOTOR CREDIT CORPORATION for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask TOYOTA MOTOR CREDIT CORPORATION directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated decisioning model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require reinstatement of a human-review and escalation path before automated adverse actions take effect, and clear, specific reasons plus an accessible appeal route for affected customers.
actions.basis: Consumer Duty — Consumer Support & Understanding outcomes

clusters.name: Mortgages — Insurance, Newrez, Shellpoint — Trouble during payment process
sample_narratives:
Subject : Mishandling of Loan Recast Request by NewRez Details : I purchased a home XXXX months ago, and my mortgage servicing was transferred to NewRez. 

On XX/XX/year>, I deposited {$80000.00} to initiate a loan recast. 

Since then, I have received contradictory and inaccurate information from multiple NewRez representatives : On XX/XX/year>, a representative told me the recast was complete. 

The online account still reflected the old payment amount. 

The automated system says the recast is complete, but live agents say it is still pending. 

I was told I must pay a {$250.00} processing fee, but representatives gave conflicting instructions ( deduct from deposit vs. pay separately ). 

A supervisor promised a XXXX email, but I never received it. 

On XX/XX/year>, I was told a mailed acknowledgment would arrive next week. 

Impact : I have been misled repeatedly, wasting significant time and effort. 

The recast process has been delayed for over XXXX weeks despite my timely deposit. 

The companys contradictory communication has caused confusion and financial uncertainty. 

Requested Resolution : Immediate completion of the recast with correct monthly payment adjustment. 

Written confirmation of the recast terms. 

Assurance that fees and deposits are properly applied. 

Investigation into XXXX customer service practices.

We pay our homeowners insurance through escrow. Despite this, our homeowners insurance policy was canceled due to non-payment. The insurance company states they invoiced the active lender at the time but payment was not received. This resulted in a lapse in coverage through no fault of our own. Although no damage occurred, this exposed us to significant financial risk. 

We are requesting a full investigation, written proof of timely payment ( including cleared payment documentation ), and confirmation that escrow was being properly administered. 

The active lender at the time was United Wholesale Mortgage company prior to transferring our mortgage to XXXX XXXX XXXX. 

All attempts to contact United wholesale Mortgage have been unsuccessful and an automated system tells us to speak to our current lender, but the issue was in the past with XXXX XXXX.

Company : Shellpoint Mortgage Servicing ( Newrez LLC ) Loan Number : XXXX Property Address : XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX Shellpoint Mortgage Servicing serviced my loan for the entire XXXX tax year. 
My loan was transferred to XXXX in XX/XX/XXXX. 

I need my XXXX Form 1098 ( Mortgage Interest Statement ) for tax filing purposes. 

When I call Shellpoint/Newrez , the automated system will not allow me to reach a representative because my loan number is no longer active in their system. The call disconnects automatically. 

Since Shellpoint serviced my loan during the XXXX tax year, they are legally required to provide my XXXX Form 1098. 

I am requesting that Shellpoint provide my XXXX Form 1098 immediately.

clusters.name: Consumer Credit — Promotional, Review, Denial — Charged fees or interest you didn't expect
sample_narratives: I am requesting CFPB investigation into Navy Federal Credit Union for unlawful and deceptive lending practices after it denied my {$3000.00} personal loan application based on vague, generic credit report information that Navy Federal did not independently verify, validate, or meaningfully review. The denial appears to have been generated through automated system decisioning rather than a lawful, individualized underwriting analysis, in violation of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1681 ), the Equal Credit Opportunity Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1691 ), and the Consumer Financial Protection Acts prohibition on Unfair, Deceptive, or Abusive Acts or Practices ( 12 U.S.C. 5531 ). Navy Federal failed to disclose specific tradelines, verified data points, dates, or material deficiencies relied upon, while presenting the systems output as a factual basis for denial despite its inaccuracy. This conduct is unfair and deceptive because it relies on unverified data, abusive because it deprives the consumer of the ability to dispute or correct alleged information, and unlawful because it masks automated decisioning as legitimate underwriting. These actions suggest systemic compliance failures, improper reliance on third-party or algorithmic data, and disregard for statutory accuracy and disclosure obligations, warranting immediate CFPB supervisory and enforcement review.

I am submitting this complaint regarding Upgrades handling of my account and a hardship request. 
On or around XX/XX/XXXX, I contacted Upgrade to request hardship assistance on my account due to financial difficulty. During that call, I was told that an investigation or review would be completed and that someone would call me back to discuss available options. I was led to believe my account was under review and that I would be contacted before any negative action was taken. 
I never received a call back, email, or letter from Upgrade. No one followed up with me, and I was not informed that the review had been completed or denied. I made multiple attempts to get help after this and was unable to get clear information or assistance.

Despite being told my hardship request was under review, Upgrade reported my account as 30 days late on or around [ date ]. When I contacted Upgrade after the late payment was reported, I was told that nothing could be done and that no hardship options were available, even though I had never been contacted to complete or discuss the investigation I was promised. 
I believe Upgrade failed to complete the investigation in a timely manner and failed to communicate with me as promised. Had I been informed that no assistance would be provided, I would have taken other steps to avoid a late payment being reported. I am requesting that Upgrade review this matter, provide documentation of the hardship investigation they claim to have conducted, and correct any inaccurate or unfair credit reporting that resulted from their lack of follow-up.

case_records.narrative:
I entered into a deferred interest financing agreement with PatientFi in connection with a service provided through XXXX. 

While a promotional expiration date was disclosed at the outset, it was not reasonably accessible or visible throughout the life of the loan. The deadline was primarily buried within account statements rather than clearly displayed in my account dashboard. While the promotional expiration date may have appeared on the general account homepage, it was not displayed within the specific loan detail page where a borrower would reasonably expect to find key loan terms. This inconsistency made it difficult to reliably track the deadline. They told me the date was removed after the deadline passed, so I could have kept paying the 30 % interest without any idea.

At no point did I receive any reminder, notification, or alert that the promotional expiration date was approaching. This lack of notice is particularly concerning given the severe consequence of missing the deadline.

I paid the balance in full within 11 days after the promotional expiration, demonstrating clear intent to comply with the promotional terms and always paid my bill on time. Despite this, Patien

consumer_duty.rationale: Customers cannot reach a human to question or reverse an automated decision — an unreasonable barrier to support and redress.

Truist would not allow my payment to process through online systems and in a timely fashion giving an error message stating that Truist was unable to verify that I was the owner of the account I was using to pay my mortgage, even though the legal name on my loan and the name on my checking account are the exact same. I was forced to call and process the payment through their automated system on XX/XX/year>, and then spoke to a support individual who stated that now that I am paid in full, I can set up a biweekly payment through the online system. I attempted to login online to sign up for biweekly payments and once again was met with the same ownership validation message on XX/XX/year>. This all seems like an elaborate rouse to force you into opening only a Truist bank account or an opportunity to charge late fees due to the fact that " they can't validate ownership '' when I've now paid my mortgage XXXX times from the exact same account.

In XX/XX/2025, my wife and I contacted Amerisave Mortgage in good faith to seek temporary assistance due to an unexpected, short-term financial strain. At that time, we were explicitly advised that we would need to be at least XXXX months delinquent before we could apply for any available assistance. We had never missed a payment and were not at risk of default. 

Relying on this guidance, and as is reflected in our hardship statement, we withheld payments solely to meet the stated eligibility requirement and submitted our mortgage assistance application immediately upon meeting that requirement in late XX/XX/2025. 

In late XX/XX/2025, we received and accepted a loan modification agreement and entered a XXXX XXXX XXXX. We made every required payment on time throughout the trial period, which concluded in XX/XX/2025, and we have continued making all payments as agreed since then. 

Despite our full compliance, Amerisave reported up to XXXX consecutive months of missed mortgage payments, including multiple entries reported as over 120 days delinquent. These delinquencies occurred only because we followed Amerisaves instructions during the loss-mitigation process. The account now shows as Current, but the historical delinquency reporting remains and has caused severe and ongoing credit damage. 

We have attempted to resolve this directly with Amerisave and through disputes with all three credit bureaus. The bureau disputes were verified as accurate based on automated system data and did not address the servicers role in directing payment withholding during the modification process. 

We are requesting that Amerisave review the full account history, acknowledge that the delinquency reporting resulted from servicer-directed loss mitigation activity, and correct or remove the associated negative credit reporting, as it does not fairly or accurately reflect our payment behavior.

assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 8 consumers affected by the same pattern at Mr. Cooper Group Inc. and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: automated system.
The pattern repeats across 8 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Repeated automated detriment calls into question whether the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes (cross-cutting duty to avoid foreseeable harm).

hypotheses.mechanism: Automated underwriting / affordability logic or an automated rate-switching process appears to be producing detriment without a clear, individualised rationale.
hypotheses.evidence: 8 complaints showing automated underwriting and rate-switch cues; concentrated at Mr. Cooper Group Inc., TRUIST FINANCIAL CORPORATION, AMERISAVE MORTGAGE CORPORATION.

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to Mr. Cooper Group Inc. for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask Mr. Cooper Group Inc. directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated decisioning model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

clusters.name: Consumer Credit — Refund, Year, Affirm — Problem when making payments
sample_narratives: I have filed multiple complaints about king of cash not giving me my refund of money that was taken from my account after a loan was never funded this has been going on since XXXX of 2025 they responded to my last complaint that a refund was submitted XX/XX/XXXX I never received that refund I tried to call on the phone no one can ever transfer me to the finance department to figure out where my refund is and I have been getting the run around since XXXX of this year it's seems to me making a a complaint to you all does nothing because they sit here and they lie and you all go for it with no recourse or reaction this doesn't make any sense I have sent emails and made phone calls problem still not resolved

On XX/XX/year>, I made a purchase through Affirm with XXXX XXXX XXXX for approximately {$270.00}. A duplicate order was created in error, and XXXX XXXX XXXX canceled XXXX of the purchases. The canceled order was refunded in full. 

On XX/XX/year>, Affirm applied XXXX adjustments of {$130.00} and {$130.00}, which together total the full {$270.00} refund amount. However, instead of applying the full refund to the canceled loan and closing it, Affirm split the refund across XXXX separate loans. As a result, I now have XXXX active loans and am being required to make XXXX payments within days of each other for a purchase that was canceled. 

I contacted Affirm multiple times via chat on XX/XX/year>, and was transferred between several agents and departments over more than an hour. I provided confirmation that the merchant canceled the duplicate order and that the refund emails state the refund was processed. Despite this, Affirm representatives stated the system automatically applied the refund to multiple loans and that they are unable to move or correct the funds once applied. 

A supervisor confirmed that the refund was split due to an automated system process and stated that because the adjustments were already applied, Affirm can not correct the loan balances. I was told a push notification was allegedly sent on XX/XX/year>, allowing me to manually match the refund, but I never received this notification and was not given the opportunity to apply the refund myself. I do receive Affirm emails and SMS notifications, and no notice explaining this process was provided to me. 

As a result of XXXX system error, I am being billed for XXXX loans instead of having the canceled loan fully voided. I did not authorize XXXX loans, did not agree to split refund adjustments, and can not afford duplicate payments for a canceled transaction. I am requesting correction of my account so the canceled loan is closed and the balance is adjusted to reflect that I owe {$0.00} on the cance

This loan is repaid through my payroll department. XXXX payments are {$140.00} per pay period. On XX/XX/year>, my payroll department erroneously sent my entire check to XXXX ; {$140.00} plus an additional {$1900.00}. On XXXX XXXX, the representative that I spoke with acknowledged the overpayment and advised that the refund would be processed. The representative explained that the timeframe would be outside of the XXXX days that was their original policy. I understood and that. I called the company XX/XX/year>, and their automated system told me that a refund in the amount of {$1900.00} was processed. I have called every weekday since XX/XX/year> and I have not gotten any information outside of what the automated system provided. I have requested a supervisor call back on each phone call. I received a call from a supervisor, XXXX, on XX/XX/year>. She did not offer any new information, so I requested that a XXXX give me a call back. That has not happened as yet. The major concern that I have is being unable to get any information or speak with anyone with any authority. I have been getting ran around, to chase my own money that was CLEARLY a mistake. My plan for now is to stop my payroll payments until I receive my refund. Please help.

clusters.name: Mortgages — Payoff, Cooper, Active — Trouble during payment process
sample_narratives: I paid off my HELOC mortgage with Mr. Cooper. They sent me only a photocopy of the lien release. The county requires the original wetink notarized release. When I call Mr. Cooper, I can not reach a representative, and the automated system says it will take 39 months to send the original. This delay prevents me from clearing the title. In addition, I asked for a payoff quote, but they gave me a quote a day later than the servicable date so I added extra money to cover whatever interest was being added. They have not paid difference back to me.

I had already prepaid interest through XX/XX/XXXX, and as of XX/XX/XXXX, my principal balance was only one cent. Because mortgage interest accrues solely on the outstanding principal, no additional interest should have accrued after that date regardless of when the payoff was processed. However, UWMs automated system generated two different payoff statementsone in XXXX and another in XXXX significantly different refund amounts. The XX/XX/XXXX payoff statement showed that UWM owed me {$21000.00}, while the XX/XX/XXXX payoff statement showed only {$16000.00}, a difference of about {$5500.00}. The sole change was the interest to payoff date, even though my principal remained effectively zero. This discrepancy strongly suggests that UWMs system misapplied its standard payoff logic to your unique situation, calculating additional interest that could not have accrued.

The communications with UWM show that I made good-faith efforts to clarify and resolve the issue. After paying down the loan to a near-zero balance, I requested guidance on how to complete the payoff. UWMs customer service initially directed me to call, and upon doing so, I was told to generate a payoff statement. The XXXX payoff statement contained instructions only for borrowers who owe funds to UWM, such as wiring payment, but offered no direction for cases where the lender owes a refund to the borrower. Because of that lack of guidance, the payoff process was not initiated until I followed up again in XXXX, at which point UWM recalculated the payoff amount using its automated system and reduced your refund. UWM later confirmed through written messages that the payoff was processed only after your XXXX call, that the refund was {$16000.00}, and that its system calculates interest through the payoff date. However, the company never addressed the fact that no additional interest could accrue on a {$0.00} principal. Their explanation that the payoff includes the interest to the payoff date is not factually

I am submitting this complaint as a formal escalation of a previously reported issue from XXXX XXXX, when my mortgage loan was transferred from XXXX XXXX to Mr. Cooper. At that time, Mr. Coopers payment portal failed to properly display my active loan. I filed a CFPB complaint, but the companys response did not address the root cause.

Although I was later able to make several successful payments, the identical issue has now recurred as of XX/XX/XXXX. When logging into Mr. Coopers payment portal, my current active loan does not appear. Instead, the system displays an older loan that had previously been serviced by Mr. Cooper.

This presents serious operational and consumer access concerns : 1. * * Account Access Impairment : * * The failure to display the correct active loan prevents me from making timely payments through the online system. In XXXX, this forced me to mail a payment without confirmation or idempotency safeguards.

2. * * Data Integrity and System Controls : * * The recurrence of obsolete loan data in place of the active account suggests unresolved backend data governance, merger integration, or quality assurance deficiencies. Mr. Cooper merged with XXXX XXXX, and the persistence of outdated account records raises questions regarding database synchronization and internal controls.

3. * * Lack of Effective Human Escalation : * * When the automated system fails, there is no reliable path to reach a live representative without passing flawed verification mechanisms. This creates a barrier to servicing an active mortgage obligation.

In XX/XX/XXXX, NewRez LLC / Shellpoint Mortgage Servicing issued a payoff statement for my HELOC that included all required fees ( {$83.00} ). Based on this payoff quote, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX paid the loan in full so they could assume second lien position. 

After receiving full payoff funds, NewRez/Shellpoint issued me a refund for an overpayment, which included the same {$83.00} fee that had already been quoted and collected. Despite being paid in full, the account was not closed and the lien was not released. 

In XX/XX/XXXX, I discovered that the lien was still active and preventing my home sale from proceeding. I was then required to pay the {$83.00} fee a second time to initiate closure of the account. The account was subsequently closed effective XX/XX/XXXX, instead of XX/XX/XXXX when the payoff was actually completed.

NewRez/Shellpoint is now treating this as a new payoff and is delaying the lien release by 4060 days. However, their automated system indicates the lien release was already sent to the county on XX/XX/XXXX, with an expected release date of XX/XX/XXXX, and document availability on XX/XX/XXXX. Their representatives have been unable to confirm or explain this dis

XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( I originally closed my loan with XXXX  XXXX Home Loans ) serviced my loan until I refinanced on XX/XX/XXXX. I am now trying to refinance again and when my title company pulled a XXXX history the release of XXXX by XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX was not filed with the county. When I view the XXXX XXXX recorders office, there is a lien from XXXX XXXX ( they transferred my loan to XXXX ) but there is not a release of XXXX. I tried calling XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX at XXXX on Tuesday, XX/XX/XXXX and several times on Thursday, XXXX and entered my social security XXXX  as well as the loan XXXX from my payoff and the automated system states they can not find a past or present loan with that information so to call back when I have the correct info. I am stuck in this loop and it does not allow me to speak to a a customer service representative. I found an email address ( XXXX ) for customer inquiries and emailed them on XX/XX/XXXX and attached my payoff quote from XXXX that reflected my name, full property address and their loan number and received a response on XXXX stating they are unable to locate an account under the property address and/or borrower information provided. On XXXX I

Mortgage ProsXXXX refinance and cash out. 
Payoff auto loans and credit cards. One vehicle paid off with a payoff amount from lender the other payoff amount was short due to NOT obtaining a payoff. Mortgage Pros used amount on credit bureau from XXXX. It was noted that this is their standard practice and are not required by CFPB to obtain a payoff from the lender. The Assistant VP informed me that they went above and beyound thier duty to obtain a payoff amount when they normally use a credit reporting bureau. I was left with an excesive amount to payoff this vehicle, {$830.00} ( approx. ). Thhis was on XXXX. 
On or about XXXX I was in contact with the Loan officer, XXXX XXXX, her supervisor, XXXX XXXX and another associate, XXXX XXXX about this same issue. I was told that Mortgage Pros can not just pull a number out of the air. They have to obtain a payoff amount. It was also discovered that they did try to contact XXXX XXXX but was unable to get through. XXXX XXXX does not have any record of Mortgage Pros requesting a payoff. assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 7 consumers affected by the same pattern at Mr. Cooper Group Inc. and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: automated system.
The pattern repeats across 7 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Repeated automated detriment calls into question whether the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes (cross-cutting duty to avoid foreseeable harm).

hypotheses.mechanism: Automated underwriting / affordability logic or an automated rate-switching process appears to be producing detriment without a clear, individualised rationale.
hypotheses.evidence: 7 complaints showing automated underwriting and rate-switch cues; concentrated at Mr. Cooper Group Inc., United Shore Financial Services, LLC, Shellpoint Partners, LLC.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to Mr. Cooper Group Inc. for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask Mr. Cooper Group Inc. directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated decisioning model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.

clusters.name: Student Lending — Mohela, Servicing, Reporting — Dealing with your lender or servicer
sample_narratives:
I am filing this complaint regarding inaccurate delinquency reporting and servicing failures by MOHELA related to my student loan account. 
Auto-debit was requested and established in XX/XX/XXXX using my XXXX XXXX account ending in XXXX ( see attached statement for reference ). My account was set up for automatic monthly payments. 
Between XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX, payments were not drafted from my account. During this time, I did not receive any failed draft notifications, returned payment alerts, or delinquency warnings. I was unaware that payments were not being processed. 
In XX/XX/XXXX, after learning of similar issues from a coworker, I logged into my account to review my status and discovered that payments had not been deducted as expected. Only after logging in did I begin receiving delinquency email notifications. It appeared that notifications resumed or were triggered following my login. 
Once I realized the issue, I immediately sent manual payment to bring the account fully current. I also re-established auto-debit using a different bank account, my XXXX account ending in XXXX, to prevent further issues. 
Despite being enrolled in auto-debit and not receiving failed draft notifications during XXXX XXXX, MOHELA reported XXXX account as delinquent to the credit bureaus. The account is now fully current and remains in good standing. 
I have attempted to resolve this issue directly with MOHELA on XXXX separate occasions prior to filing this complaint, requesting investigation and correction of the delinquency reporting. My requests were denied. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, at approximately XXXX XXXX, I spoke with a MOHELA representative identified as XXXX ( ID : XXXX ). During this call, the representative raised his voice, attempted to belittle me regarding monitoring my auto-debit, blamed me for not checking emails, denied the possibility of a system error, and initially refused to escalate my call to a supervisor, stating that a supervisor would tell me the same thing. I w

Description of Incident : I am requesting a full discharge of my three student loans due to deceptive and predatory servicing practices by EdFinancial.

For several years, I was enrolled in EdFinancials automatic payment system. I was led to believe this was a good-faith method to satisfy my debt. However, EdFinancial structured these automated payments to cover interest only, allocating {$0.00} to the principal balance for every single payment made.

By luring me into an automated system that resulted in zero principal reduction without clear disclosure, EdFinancial has engaged in unfair and deceptive acts that effectively created a " debt trap. '' This practice of forced negative amortization has caused me significant financial harm by keeping me in a cycle of perpetual interest payments while my total balance remains unchanged.

Because EdFinancial failed in its duty to provide transparent repayment options and instead chose to maximize interest collection through deceptive automation, I am requesting that these loans be discharged in full.

Desired Resolution : Full discharge of all three loans associated with account username XXXX, a correction of my payment history to reflect principal reduction, and a formal investigation into EdFinancial 's automated payment allocation practices.

I am submitting this complaint because MOHELA, acting as the federal student loan servicer for the Department of Education, continues to report inaccurate and legally unverifiable late payment information on my credit reports despite receiving multiple disputes and formal requests for verification. 

The accounts involved in this complaint are : MOHELA / XXXX XXXX MOHELA / XXXX  XXXX MOHELA / XXXX  XXXX MOHELA / XXXX  XXXX These student loan accounts themselves are not being disputed. My complaint specifically concerns the late payment reporting associated with these accounts, which is inaccurate, inconsistent across credit bureaus, and unsupported by the documentation required under federal consumer protection laws. 

I have repeatedly notified MOHELA and the credit reporting agencies that the late payment data associated with these student loans is materially inconsistent across XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. The same accounts are reporting different delinquency severities and timelines depending on the bureau reviewing the data. In some cases, the accounts show conflicting XXXX, XXXX, and XXXX late designations for the same periods of time. 

A single federal student loan can not legitimately generate multiple conflicting delinquency histories across credit bureaus. The presence of these contradictions demonstrates that the information being furnished does not meet the legal standard of maximum possible accuracy required under federal law. 

In addition to the conflicting delinquency timelines, the accounts also reflect inconsistent reporting regarding payment history, activity dates, and other servicing information. These discrepancies indicate that the information being furnished is not being verified through actual servicing records. 

Under the Fair Credit Reporting Act, once a consumer disputes information, the furnisher of that information has a legal obligation to conduct a reasonable investigation using the underlying records that support the reporting. 

Furthermo

Auto-debit was requested and established in XX/XX/XXXX using my XXXX XXXX account ending in XXXX ( see attached statement for reference ). My account was set up for automatic monthly payments. 
Between XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX, payments were not drafted from my account. During this time, I did not receive any failed draft notifications, returned payment alerts, or delinquency warnings. I was unaware that payments were not being processed. 
In XX/XX/XXXX, after learning of similar issues from a coworker, I logged into my account to review my status and discovered that payments had not been deducted as expected. Only after logging in did I begin receiving delinquency email notifications. It appeared that notifications resumed or were triggered following my login. 
Once I realized the issue, I immediately sent manual payment to bring the account fully current. I also re-established auto-debit using a different bank account, my XXXX account ending in XXXX, to prevent further issues.

case_records.issue: Dealing with your lender or servicer
case_records.narrative:
Description of Incident : I am requesting a full discharge of my three student loans due to deceptive and predatory servicing practices by EdFinancial.

For several years, I was enrolled in EdFinancials automatic payment system. I was led to believe this was a good-faith method to satisfy my debt. However, EdFinancial structured these automated payments to cover interest only, allocating {$0.00} to the principal balance for every single payment made.

By luring me into an automated system that resulted in zero principal reduction without clear disclosure, EdFinancial has engaged in unfair and deceptive acts that effectively created a " debt trap. '' This practice of forced negative amortization has caused me significant financial harm by keeping me in a cycle of perpetual interest payments while my total balance remains unchanged.

Because EdFinancial failed in its duty to provide transparent repayment options and instead chose to maximize interest collection through deceptive automation, I am requesting that these loans be discharged in full.

Desired Resolution : Full discharge of all three loans associated with account username XXXX, a correction of my payment history to reflect

I am submitting this complaint because MOHELA, acting as the federal student loan servicer for the Department of Education, continues to report inaccurate and legally unverifiable late payment information on my credit reports despite receiving multiple disputes and formal requests for verification. 

The accounts involved in this complaint are : MOHELA / XXXX XXXX MOHELA / XXXX  XXXX MOHELA / XXXX  XXXX MOHELA / XXXX  XXXX These student loan accounts themselves are not being disputed. My complaint specifically concerns the late payment reporting associated with these accounts, which is inaccurate, inconsistent across credit bureaus, and unsupported by the documentation required under federal consumer protection laws. 

I have repeatedly notified MOHELA and the credit reporting agencies that the late payment data associated with these student loans is materially inconsistent across XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. The same accounts are reporting different delinquency severities and timelines depending on the bureau reviewing the data. In some cases, the accounts show conflicting XXXX, XXXX, and XXXX late designations for the same periods of time. 

A single federal student loan can not legitima

case_records.issue: Dealing with your lender or servicer
case_records.narrative:
I am an existing, grandfathered borrower enrolled in the XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX ( XXXX ) repayment plan. I submitted a timely, manual income-driven repayment ( XXXX ) Request form to my servicer, MOHELA, XXXX selecting " recertification '' and the XXXX plan XXXX My form included proof of current income. 

On XX/XX/year>, MOHELA 's automated system incorrectly denied my recertification, erroneously claiming I requested the " lowest monthly payment '' and citing an unrelated federal injunction. I contacted MOHELA on XX/XX/year> and spoke with a resolutions XXXX ( First name : XXXX, Employee ID XXXX XXXXXXXX ). The XXXX refused to manually correct the system error. 

Under XXXX XXXX XXXX, existing XXXX borrowers who no longer demonstrate a partial financial hardship are entitled to remain in the XXXX plan, with their maximum monthly payment capped at the permanent standard XXXX amount calculated when they entered the plan. MOHELA is illegally treating my recertification as a new application and refusing to apply the federally mandated cap.

case_records.issue: Dealing with your lender or servicer
case_records.narrative:
On XX/XX/year>, I made a student loan payment of {$180.00} through the Sallie Mae mobile application. The payment was made from my XXXX XXXX XXXX account and applied to XXXX # XXXX. 
Sallie Mae has since reversed the payment claiming insufficient funds and marked my account as delinquent. 
I contacted my bank and obtained confirmation that sufficient funds were available at the time the payment was initiated. I have documentation verifying this. 
I attempted to resolve the issue through Sallie Maes customer service chat multiple times. The same representative connected to my chats but did not respond and disconnected the sessions, preventing me from resolving the issue through normal support channels. 
Because the funds were available and the payment was properly authorized through the Sallie Mae application, this appears to be a payment processing or servicing error by Sallie Mae. 
This issue is particularly concerning because I have experienced similar servicing problems with Sallie Mae in the past. For example, Sallie Mae previously attempted to collect payments while my loan was in in-school deferment. When I initially contacted customer service about that issue, they disputed

assessment.why_concerning: LOW severity: 5 consumers affected by the same pattern at MOHELA and others, and it is persistent and unresolved. Left unaddressed it risks foreseeable harm at scale before any individual complaint is resolved.
ai_rationale: Complaints explicitly reference automation: automated system.
The pattern repeats across 5 complaints — more consistent with a systematic automated process than with isolated human error.

consumer_duty.rationale: Repeated automated detriment calls into question whether the journey is designed to deliver good outcomes (cross-cutting duty to avoid foreseeable harm).

hypotheses.mechanism: A recently deployed or changed automated decisioning process appears to be producing the recurring detriment seen in these complaints.
hypotheses.evidence: 5 complaints showing repeating automated-handling cues; concentrated at MOHELA, EdFinancial Services, SLM CORPORATION.

hypotheses.mechanism: If the automated model relies on variables that act as proxies for protected characteristics (e.g. postcode, name), outcomes may be indirectly discriminatory even without intent.
hypotheses.evidence: Category is exposed to proxy-based discrimination; decisions issued without reasons make disparate impact hard to detect.

actions.action: Issue a formal information request to MOHELA for documentation of any automated decisioning model in this journey — model purpose, version/change log over the affected period, validation reports, and decline/false-positive/exit-rate metrics.
Ask MOHELA directly: did you deploy or materially change an automated decisioning model in the last 6–12 months, and what consumer-outcome testing preceded it?
Require a Consumer Duty outcomes assessment for the affected journey, evidencing that customers receive good outcomes and fair value.
Require a disparate-impact / Equality Act impact assessment of the model across protected groups, with evidence the firm tests for and mitigates proxy discrimination.
actions.basis: Equality Act 2010 + Consumer Duty (avoid foreseeable harm)

clusters.name: Student Lending — Notice, Mohela, Access — Dealing with your lender or servicer
companies: Servicer under contract with Federal Student Aid
sample_narratives:
On XX/XX/XXXX, I logged into my IRS account to check the status of my amended refund and saw a transaction code showing my refund was applied to a non-IRS debt. This was the FIRST time I learned anything about an offset. I immediately called the Treasury XXXX XXXX ( XXXX ), and the automated system informed me that my refund had been sent to the U.S. Department of Education ( DOE ). I received no advance notice whatsoever. 

My XXXX federal tax refund was taken on XX/XX/XXXX without the legally required XXXX Notice of Intent to XXXX. Under 31 U.S.C. 3716 ( a ), 31 C.F.R. 285.5, and 34 C.F.R. 30.22, DOE must send a current written notice BEFORE each offset year, giving borrowers updated debt information and the opportunity to dispute or request a hearing. 

Because I received no notice, I was denied all due-process rights. 

Later that same day ( XX/XX/XXXX ), I contacted DOEs Default Resolution Group. DOE staff admitted that the ONLY notice they claim to have issued was from XXXX. A notice from XXXX does NOT authorize a XXXX offset. Federal law requires new notice before EACH tax year an offset occurs. 

DOE representatives gave incorrect and misleading information : One said, Theres nothing you can do now. 
Another said, A notice from XXXX still goes into effect. 
A supervisor ( XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX ) incorrectly said refunds cant be returned unless facing eviction or foreclosure. 
The supervisor also said HIPAA prevents them from updating addresses, which is completely irrelevant to federal student loan servicing.

Staff suggested email notice could apply, but I received no email either. 

The Treasury XXXX letter I eventually received was mailed to my correct address, proving IRS/Treasury had my updated address. DOE failed to use the same federal address databases, violating due process ( XXXX v. XXXX  ).

Throughout the XXXX calendar year, MOHELA failed to provide requisite billing statements, account disclosures, or any official correspondence regarding the status of my debt. Despite this lack of notice, I exercised due diligence by attempting to access my account through MOHELAs official portal ; however, I was consistently barred by a technical error stating that no account existed for my Social Security numbera credential that was fully functional prior to XXXX. 

Furthermore, my attempts to mitigate these issues via telephone were ignored ; I was subjected to hold times exceeding XXXX hours and MOHELA failed to honor multiple requests for a return call via their automated system. Despite these documented servicing failures and my ongoing status as a Public Service Loan Forgiveness ( PSLF ) candidate with a qualifying payment history, MOHELA unilaterally reported a balance on my credit profile on XX/XX/XXXX, without prior notification or the opportunity for resolution.

Subject : Complaint Regarding Inability to Access Account or Pay Remaining Student Loan Balance ( MOHELA ) To Whom It May Concern, I am submitting this complaint regarding MOHELA and my inability to access my student loan account or make payment on a remaining balance of {$150.00}, despite repeated good-faith efforts to resolve the issue. 

For months, I have attempted to pay this remaining balance, but I have been unable to access my account or reach anyone who can assist me. Specifically : Online account access : When I attempt to log in or verify my identity online, the system reports that my name and Social Security number do not match their records. I have only one legal name and Social Security number, and I am entering them correctly. 

Phone system : When I call MOHELA, the automated system requires me to enter the same identifying information. After doing so, it again states that my information does not match their records, preventing me from reaching a live representative for assistance. 

Email attempts : I have emailed MOHELA requesting help, but I was only redirected back to the same phone number that I can not access due to the identity verification issue. 

Federal student loan support : I also contacted the main federal student loan service to report that I do not have access to my MOHELA account and can not make payment. This did not resolve the issue. 

Cashiers check : As a final attempt to resolve the matter, I mailed MOHELA a cashiers check for the full remaining balance. The check was returned to me and not processed. 

Despite my repeated efforts to pay this debt in full, my credit score has been negatively affected for nine months due to this unresolved balance, resulting in nine missed payment docks. 

I am requesting CFPB assistance to : Ensure MOHELA properly verifies my identity and restores access to my account, OR Accept and process my payment for the full remaining balance immediately, and Correct all nine months of negative credit rep

clusters.name: Debt Collection — Escrow, Paid, Payoff — Attempts to collect debt not owed
sample_narratives:
I am attempting to obtain written payoff statements for judgment accounts being handled by XXXX XXXX. 

XXXX  XXXX  automated system instructs consumers to request payoff statements via email. When I follow that instruction and submit the request by email, I receive a response stating that I must call instead. 

When I call XXXX XXXX, I am repeatedly told to allow XXXX business days for the payoff statements to be issued. I have received this same response for over a week, despite multiple follow-ups, and no payoff statements have been provided. 

Most recently, I was advised that XXXX XXXX does not provide payoff letters as part of its business practice and that I should calculate my own interest based on the judgment. 

This refusal and ongoing delay prevents me from resolving or satisfying the judgments and interferes with my ability to manage and resolve the debt. I am requesting that XXXX XXXX immediately provide written payoff statements that clearly state the total amount due, including any interest and fees, and explain why these payoff statements have not been provided despite repeated requests.

QWR and DVR to disclose status of account receivable due from me. clusters.name: Student Lending — Asked, Stated, Rate — Dealing with your lender or servicer
sample_narratives:
XXXX XXXX XXXX est I have missed approximately 40+ minutes of work, at the time I am beginning to type this complaint to the CFPB.

I recorded that call experience with Mohela, and the link to that call is here : XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX I have stored that filed on a secure server, hosted by XXXX, on the back-end of a website that displays some of my health science studies.

Audio files are typically too large to attach to the CFPB platform.

Here is a typed summary of what can be witnessed in the recorded call evidence, which demonstrates decades of customer abuse, committed against myself, and millions of other students, over the years : 1. Because I am currently utilizing a Rate Reducation Program, I am punished in a variety of ways that cost me excessive time, money, and lost wages. One of these ways is that I am no longer allowed to make payments using the customer portal online, and I am forced to miss work, and call in, which is always a long, time consuming, ridiculous process.

2. I called a valid number, but the automated system intentionally sends me to a department that is completely irrelevant to any service I would need. Today, it forwarded me to a
Federal Student Loan Only Payment Dept, and I do NOT have ANY Federal Student Loans with Mohela, or in existence, at all, anywhere.

3. I was put on hold twice, at least, for excessive spans of time.

4. I had to be transferred at least once.

5. Their system had lots of technical difficulties, creating more delays, and the agent claimed that this is a common problem.

6. I was encouraged to wait until after my program expires before reapplying, which could cause a lapse in my program.

7. I asked what my Interest Rate was prior to the current Rate Reduction program, and the agent reports that is information she can not give me.

8. I asked what my Interest Rate was on XXXX XXXX XXXX, and the agent reports that she can not give me that, nor is she able, or allowed, to give me any historical interest rate data.

9.

I called XXXX on XX/XX/year> at XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX. But the automated system stated " unable to find your records '' based off my phone number and social security number. I was on the phone with representative " XXXX for XXXX minutes. '' I asked why my XXXX loan for XXXX University XXXX which has the first disbursement amount of XXXX wasn't configured to the balance for XXXX ( because XXXX was a cancelled amount ). But I am not in forbearance. I have been in XXXX. I asked her how am I going to pay, or try to pay when you put me in forbearance and the interest is so high? I AM IN XXXX. I have been in XXXX the past few years. I AM NOT IN XXXX. So now, a dispersed loan of XXXX is now, according to their records is XXXX because of forbearance interest. So all this time, they had me in forbearance and not XXXX. So the principal balance XXXX from an original disbursement of XXXX is now XXXX??? Ask her how am I ever going to pay when the interest is more than the amount borrowed because you based it off of forberance???

I have student loans with Aidvantage. I went to school for XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX at XXXX XXXX XXXX. I was on track to XXXX  until the school closed before i could enter my last quarter of my " senior '' year. They offered teach out programs but they were all 3x the amount of schooling than what i had left at XXXX since the credits didn't carry over completely. The degrees that were offered were also lower end degrees that weren't close to the degree i wanted initially from XXXX. They were also extremely more expensive at the same rate. So i didn't attend the teach out program and decided to start fresh by applying for loan forgiveness. I filled out all the paperwork and faxed + emailed to aidvantage exactly how they asked me to. I was fully eligible. They didn't deny me at first. Instead, twice they mailed me back saying that my form wasn't filled out completely and to reapply. So i sat down with a few smart businessmen and filled out again. They sent back the same message. So i contacted them and asked what the issue with my form was. When i navigated through the automated system over the phone for Aidvantage going through the loan forgiveness part, the line would ring and then just hangup. It was impossible to get into contact with them. I decided to go a completely seperate route on the automated system by asking to talk to representative about something entirely different/less important. I got right in and they transfered me to someone on the forgiveness side of things. They gave me some nonsense reason of why my form was filled out incorrrectly and asked me to send again. So i sent the same exact form i sent before and they actually took it into their system. I assume so because I never recieved that feedback like before. Months later, i contacted Student Aid and get them involved. I opened up a case and they made me believe that they were on top of it. clusters.name: Student Lending — Administrative, Months, Deferment — Dealing with your lender or servicer
sample_narratives:
My application has now remained in this final review stage for approximately two months with no estimated timeframe for completion. During this period, I have submitted multiple Feedback cases through Federal Student Aid seeking clarification and assistance. These cases were all closed with automated responses directing me only to check my status online, without providing any substantive explanation, timeline, or resolution.

I have also followed the instructions provided by the Federal Student Aid Ombudsmans automated system. Despite doing so, my concerns about the prolonged delay and lack of transparency remain unresolved.

I am not disputing eligibility or requesting a change in the substantive determination of my application. I am seeking assistance with administrative processing delays, lack of meaningful communication, and the absence of a clear timeframe for completion after approval stages have been passed.

I respectfully request that this complaint be reviewed to help facilitate transparency, accountability, and completion of the final administrative steps of my TPD discharge.

On XX/XX/XXXX, I applied on XXXX for an IDR repayment plan. Afterwards, I called and spoke with ED-Financial at the hours of XXXX to XXXX where I confirmed my IDR had placed me into an administrative forbearance, as well as requesting 3 months of deferment. The representative who I spoke with ( over a recorded line ), informed me that my account was eligible for three months of deferment. 

After a few months, I decided to check XXXX to see the status of my student loans. I noticed my IDR had been completed, and that I had no past due balance. I was very confused on why XXXX wasn't updating. XXXX claimed that due to the OBBBA, accounts under IDR and SAVE are in an administrative backlog. Of course, mine were apart of that backlog. I tried to make a payment in XXXX, but found no way to complete the payment process online. I tried calling Ed-Financial when I was available after work in XXXX, but they were closed. The automated system claimed I was late on my payments. I called once again when they were open, where they informed me that my payments were 110 days late as of XX/XX/XXXX. That would mean that my first payment was due on XX/XX/XXXX. Assuming my accounts were in administrative forbearance for one month, that would be XX/XX/XXXX, plus 3 months of deferment. My first payment should've been due in XXXX of XXXX. 

When I brought this up with a representative at Ed-Financial on XX/XX/XXXX, they told me they had no record of my request for deferment. I asked them for all relevant recordings and calls log from that specific date, which they record and are required to retain. They declined.

clusters.name: Student Lending — Score, Alerts, Delinquent — Dealing with your lender or servicer
sample_narratives:
On XX/XX/XXXX, I received 4 delinquent payment alerts on my credit report from Firstmark XXXX XXXX. I called Firstmark and the automated response told me my account was current and not due until XX/XX/XXXX. On the XXXX, I called to find out from a representative that there was XXXX cents past due. However the automated system did not indicate any past due amount. My credit score dropped by XXXX points because of this. 

Between XX/XX/XXXX through XX/XX/XXXX, I received XXXX delinquent payment alerts on my credit report from Firstmark XXXX XXXX. Each time the automated call system and the online portal said my accounts were current. Payments are not due until the XXXX of each month. When I talked with a representative on the phone, they said that payment processing between Firstmark and XXXX 's bank is what was causing the delinquent alerts, even though Firstmark had the funds in their account. 

In total, my credit score went from XXXX to XXXX in a matter of 5 months. I've worked incredibly hard to maintain a health credit score. The actions of Firstmark are really unacceptable to consumers trying to manage their student debt.

clusters.name: Auto Lending — Principal, Principal Payments, Charged — Managing the loan or lease
sample_narratives:
Complaint Against Bank of America Deceptive Loan Servicing Practices I was in the process of purchasing an RV and was presented with several banks as potential lenders. One of those lenders was Bank of America. Before selecting any lender, I contacted Bank of America directly to confirm that they were a good fit and that they offered the specific loan servicing features I require. 

Prior to signing the loan documents, I explicitly discussed with Bank of America how the loan would be serviced, including how interest is calculated, how payments are applied, and the procedures for making principal-only payments and early payoff. I made it clear that the ability to make principal-only payments easily and frequently is essential to me, as I routinely make multiple payments per month and pay well above the minimum due. Bank of America confirmed that they could accommodate these needs. 

After the loan was originated and I attempted to make my first payment, I discovered that Bank of America does not allow principal-only payments to be made online. This directly contradicts what I was told prior to agreeing to the loan and was a deciding factor in my choice of lender. 

Instead, Bank of America requires customers to either : Mail in a physical payment with a written letter requesting that it be applied to principal only, hoping it is processed correctly, or Call in and navigate a lengthy automated system that does not allow for principal-only payments, then hope to reach a representative in the correct department who can manually process the payment. 

This process is unreasonable, inefficient, and risky. It forces customers to repeatedly disclose sensitive personal and account information over the phone and relies on manual handling that increases the likelihood of errors. More importantly, this structure actively discourages principal payments and appears designed to maximize interest collection rather than allow borrowers to reduce their balances efficiently. 

I belie

I attempted to make a payment on truist banks phone automated system, and it did not properly record the payment. Truist bank then charged me a fee of XXXX $ for " refunding a payment '' that did not go through. Talking with them yielded no results. A proper payment was made the day after but I am still being charged XXXX $

I have tried probably about 10 times in the last year to make principal payments or get a payoff amount for my auto loan and they do no provide the information or ability to do so. They hide this information and don't allow customers the ability to make principal payments so they can extract maximum interest charges. On their website there is NO information about your loan, interest rate, payoff balance, etc. and NO way to select principal payment in the payment system. On their app, it doesn't work most of the time and goes to an error screen, but one the rare occasion it opens the app there is still no way to find critical loan information or make principal payments. When I callied on the phone, twice it went through a automated system with no option to hear the critical loan information or make principal payments, and when you finally get directed to an " operator '' it stays on hold then disconnects. Even to get the information needed to refinance to another lender is not available on the site, app, or phone system.

clusters.name: Auto Lending — Reporting, Blocked, Vin — Incorrect information on your report
sample_narratives:
I am a three-year customer of U.S. Bank with a consistent on-time payment history. I am currently traveling XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX and have been subjected to aggressive and potentially illegal account restrictions and inaccurate credit reporting. 
The Facts : In late XX/XX/XXXX, I issued a XXXX Stop Payment order to ensure my funds cleared before the bank withdrew my payment. I intended to be a responsible partner by preventing an NSF ( Non-Sufficient Funds ) event. 
U.S. Banks automated system appears to have coded this as a permanent block. A subsequent manual payment I made on XX/XX/XXXX was also blocked by the bank. 
As soon as I was made aware of the technical failure, I sent a {$1800.00} Wire Transfer via XXXX XXXX in early XX/XX/XXXX to bring the account completely current. 
The Violations : FCRA Violation : Despite being current, my credit score recently dropped XXXX points. Per the Fair Credit Reporting Act, a lender can not report a delinquency until it is XXXX  days past due. I was never XXXX days delinquent. This is inaccurate reporting that has caused me significant financial harm. 
Denial of Access : U.S. Bank has " shadow-blocked '' my digital access. My app shows a generic " maintenance error '' that prevents me from seeing my balance, confirming my {$1800.00} wire, or knowing my XXXX due date. 
Hostile Terms : The bank is demanding " certified funds only '' for a situation caused by their own system 's inability to process my XX/XX/XXXX payment.

Upstart 's automated system is incorrectly decoding my VIN ( XXXX ) as a XXXX XXXX XXXX. The XXXX digit of my VIN is " J '', which is the federal legal identifier for a XXXX model year. I have the state-issued registration and XXXX verification XXXX it is a XXXX. 

When I attempted to correct this, representatives were verbally abusive, told me it was " my fault, '' and repeatedly hung up on me. They then allowed my application to expire today while I was actively attempting to provide proof of their internal data error. I believe my phone number has been blocked to prevent me from disputing this inaccurate information. This is a violation of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) regarding the use of accurate data.
